      12                 :            :  *
      13                 :            :  *
      14                 :            :  * INTERFACE ROUTINES
      15                 :            :  *              relation_open   - open any relation by relation OID
      16                 :            :  *              relation_openrv - open any relation specified by a RangeVar
      17                 :            :  *              relation_close  - close any relation
      18                 :            :  *              heap_open               - open a heap relation by relation OID
      19                 :            :  *              heap_openrv             - open a heap relation specified by a RangeVar
      20                 :            :  *              heap_close              - (now just a macro for relation_close)
      21                 :            :  *              heap_beginscan  - begin relation scan
      22                 :            :  *              heap_rescan             - restart a relation scan
      23                 :            :  *              heap_endscan    - end relation scan
      24                 :            :  *              heap_getnext    - retrieve next tuple in scan
      25                 :            :  *              heap_fetch              - retrieve tuple with given tid
      26                 :            :  *              heap_insert             - insert tuple into a relation
      27                 :            :  *              heap_delete             - delete a tuple from a relation
      28                 :            :  *              heap_update             - replace a tuple in a relation with another tuple
      29                 :            :  *              heap_markpos    - mark scan position
      30                 :            :  *              heap_restrpos   - restore position to marked location
      31                 :            :  *              heap_sync               - sync heap, for when no WAL has been written
      32                 :            :  *
      33                 :            :  * NOTES
      34                 :            :  *        This file contains the heap_ routines which implement
      35                 :            :  *        the POSTGRES heap access method used for all POSTGRES
      36                 :            :  *        relations.
      37                 :            :  *
      38                 :            :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
      39                 :            :  */

      93                 :            : static void
      94                 :            : initscan(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanKey key, bool is_rescan)
      95                 :            : {
      96                 :            :         bool            allow_strat;
      97                 :            :         bool            allow_sync;
      98                 :            : 
      99                 :            :         /*
     100                 :            :          * Determine the number of blocks we have to scan.
     101                 :            :          *
     102                 :            :          * It is sufficient to do this once at scan start, since any tuples added
     103                 :            :          * while the scan is in progress will be invisible to my snapshot anyway.
     104                 :            :          * (That is not true when using a non-MVCC snapshot.  However, we couldn't
     105                 :            :          * guarantee to return tuples added after scan start anyway, since they
     106                 :            :          * might go into pages we already scanned.      To guarantee consistent
     107                 :            :          * results for a non-MVCC snapshot, the caller must hold some higher-level
     108                 :            :          * lock that ensures the interesting tuple(s) won't change.)
     109                 :            :          */
     110                 :     473902 :         scan->rs_nblocks = RelationGetNumberOfBlocks(scan->rs_rd);
     111                 :            : 
     112                 :            :         /*
     113                 :            :          * If the table is large relative to NBuffers, use a bulk-read access
     114                 :            :          * strategy and enable synchronized scanning (see syncscan.c).  Although
     115                 :            :          * the thresholds for these features could be different, we make them the
     116                 :            :          * same so that there are only two behaviors to tune rather than four.
     117                 :            :          * (However, some callers need to be able to disable one or both of
     118                 :            :          * these behaviors, independently of the size of the table; also there
     119                 :            :          * is a GUC variable that can disable synchronized scanning.)
     120                 :            :          *
     121                 :            :          * During a rescan, don't make a new strategy object if we don't have to.
     122                 :            :          */

     124                 :     471559 :                 scan->rs_nblocks > NBuffers / 4)

     127                 :          9 :                 allow_sync = scan->rs_allow_sync;
     128                 :          9 :         }

     188                 :            : /*
     189                 :            :  * heapgetpage - subroutine for heapgettup()
     190                 :            :  *
     191                 :            :  * This routine reads and pins the specified page of the relation.
     192                 :            :  * In page-at-a-time mode it performs additional work, namely determining
     193                 :            :  * which tuples on the page are visible.
     194                 :            :  */

     299                 :            : 
     300                 :            : /* ----------------
     301                 :            :  *              heapgettup - fetch next heap tuple
     302                 :            :  *
     303                 :            :  *              Initialize the scan if not already done; then advance to the next
     304                 :            :  *              tuple as indicated by "dir"; return the next tuple in scan->rs_ctup,
     305                 :            :  *              or set scan->rs_ctup.t_data = NULL if no more tuples.
     306                 :            :  *
     307                 :            :  * dir == NoMovementScanDirection means "re-fetch the tuple indicated
     308                 :            :  * by scan->rs_ctup".
     309                 :            :  *
     310                 :            :  * Note: the reason nkeys/key are passed separately, even though they are
     311                 :            :  * kept in the scan descriptor, is that the caller may not want us to check
     312                 :            :  * the scankeys.
     313                 :            :  *
     314                 :            :  * Note: when we fall off the end of the scan in either direction, we
     315                 :            :  * reset rs_inited.  This means that a further request with the same
     316                 :            :  * scan direction will restart the scan, which is a bit odd, but a
     317                 :            :  * request with the opposite scan direction will start a fresh scan
     318                 :            :  * in the proper direction.  The latter is required behavior for cursors,
     319                 :            :  * while the former case is generally undefined behavior in Postgres
     320                 :            :  * so we don't care too much.
     321                 :            :  * ----------------
     322                 :            :  */
     323                 :            : static void
     324                 :            : heapgettup(HeapScanDesc scan,
     325                 :            :                    ScanDirection dir,
     326                 :            :                    int nkeys,
     327                 :            :                    ScanKey key)
     328                 :            : {
     329                 :   45799798 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
     330                 :            : 
     331                 :   45799798 :         HeapTuple       tuple = &(scan->rs_ctup);
     332                 :   45799798 :         Snapshot        snapshot = scan->rs_snapshot;
     333                 :   45799798 :         bool            backward = ScanDirectionIsBackward(dir);
     334                 :            :         BlockNumber page;
     335                 :            :         bool            finished;
     336                 :            :         Page            dp;
     337                 :            :         int                     lines;
     338                 :            :         OffsetNumber lineoff;
     339                 :            :         int                     linesleft;
     340                 :            :         ItemId          lpp;
     341                 :            : 
     342                 :            :         /*
     343                 :            :          * calculate next starting lineoff, given scan direction
     344                 :            :          */
     345                 :            :         
     346                 :            :         // -------- MirroredLock ----------
     347 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:   45799798 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_LOCK;
         [ #  # ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
     348                 :            :         
     349         [ +  - ]:   45799798 :         if (ScanDirectionIsForward(dir))
     350                 :            :         {
     351         [ +  + ]:   45799798 :                 if (!scan->rs_inited)
     352                 :            :                 {
     353                 :            :                         /*
     354                 :            :                          * return null immediately if relation is empty
     355                 :            :                          */
     356         [ +  + ]:     270614 :                         if (scan->rs_nblocks == 0)
     357                 :            :                         {
     358                 :            :                                 
     359 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      65513 :                                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_UNLOCK;
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
         [ +  + ][ +  - ]
                 [ #  # ]
     360                 :            :                                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
     361                 :            :                                 
     362 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      65513 :                                 Assert(!BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf));
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
     363                 :      65513 :                                 tuple->t_data = NULL;
     364                 :      65513 :                                 return;
     365                 :            :                         }
     366                 :     205101 :                         page = scan->rs_startblock; /* first page */
     367                 :     205101 :                         heapgetpage(scan, page);
     368                 :     205101 :                         lineoff = FirstOffsetNumber;            /* first offnum */
     369                 :     205101 :                         scan->rs_inited = true;
     370                 :     205101 :                 }
     371                 :            :                 else
     372                 :            :                 {
     373                 :            :                         /* continue from previously returned page/tuple */
     374                 :   45529184 :                         page = scan->rs_cblock;              /* current page */
     375                 :   45529184 :                         lineoff =                       /* next offnum */
     376 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:   45529184 :                                 OffsetNumberNext(ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(tuple->t_self)));

     377                 :            :                 }

     388                 :            :         {
     389         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!scan->rs_inited)
     390                 :            :                 {
     391                 :            :                         /*
     392                 :            :                          * return null immediately if relation is empty
     393                 :            :                          */
     394         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (scan->rs_nblocks == 0)

     396                 :            :

         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
                 [ #  # ]
     398                 :            :                                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
     399                 :            :

     405                 :            :                         /*

     616                 :            : /* ----------------
     617                 :            :  *              heapgettup_pagemode - fetch next heap tuple in page-at-a-time mode
     618                 :            :  *
     619                 :            :  *              Same API as heapgettup, but used in page-at-a-time mode
     620                 :            :  *
     621                 :            :  * The internal logic is much the same as heapgettup's too, but there are some
     622                 :            :  * differences: we do not take the buffer content lock (that only needs to
     623                 :            :  * happen inside heapgetpage), and we iterate through just the tuples listed
     624                 :            :  * in rs_vistuples[] rather than all tuples on the page.  Notice that
     625                 :            :  * lineindex is 0-based, where the corresponding loop variable lineoff in
     626                 :            :  * heapgettup is 1-based.
     627                 :            :  * ----------------
     628                 :            :  */
     629                 :            : static void
     630                 :            : heapgettup_pagemode(HeapScanDesc scan,

     632                 :            :                                         int nkeys,
     633                 :            :                                         ScanKey key)

     635                 :    1033864 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
     636                 :            :

     853                 :            :                 {
     854                 :     848431 :                         page++;
     855         [ +  + ]:     848431 :                         if (page >= scan->rs_nblocks)
     856                 :     120251 :                                 page = 0;

     859                 :            :                         /*
     860                 :            :                          * Report our new scan position for synchronization purposes. We
     861                 :            :                          * don't do that when moving backwards, however. That would just
     862                 :            :                          * mess up any other forward-moving scanners.
     863                 :            :                          *
     864                 :            :                          * Note: we do this before checking for end of scan so that the
     865                 :            :                          * final state of the position hint is back at the start of the
     866                 :            :                          * rel.  That's not strictly necessary, but otherwise when you run
     867                 :            :                          * the same query multiple times the starting position would shift
     868                 :            :                          * a little bit backwards on every invocation, which is confusing.
     869                 :            :                          * We don't guarantee any specific ordering in general, though.
     870                 :            :                          */
     871         [ +  + ]:     848431 :                         if (scan->rs_syncscan)
     872                 :      15453 :                                 ss_report_location(scan->rs_rd, page);
     873                 :            :                 }
     874                 :            : 
     875 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     848431 :                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_UNLOCK;
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ #  # ]
     876                 :            :                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
     877                 :            : 
     878                 :            :                 /*
     879                 :            :                  * return NULL if we've exhausted all the pages
     880                 :            :                  */

     882                 :            :                 {
     883 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     120251 :                         if (BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))

     884                 :     120251 :                                 ReleaseBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf);

     886                 :     120251 :                         scan->rs_cblock = InvalidBlockNumber;

     907                 :            : 
     908                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     909                 :            :  *                                       heap access method interface
     910                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     911                 :            :  */
     912                 :            : 
     913                 :            : /* ----------------
     914                 :            :  *              relation_open - open any relation by relation OID
     915                 :            :  *
     916                 :            :  *              If lockmode is not "NoLock", the specified kind of lock is
     917                 :            :  *              obtained on the relation.  (Generally, NoLock should only be
     918                 :            :  *              used if the caller knows it has some appropriate lock on the
     919                 :            :  *              relation already.)
     920                 :            :  *
     921                 :            :  *              An error is raised if the relation does not exist.
     922                 :            :  *
     923                 :            :  *              NB: a "relation" is anything with a pg_class entry.  The caller is
     924                 :            :  *              expected to check whether the relkind is something it can handle.
     925                 :            :  * ----------------
     926                 :            :  */
     927                 :            : Relation
     928                 :            : relation_open(Oid relationId, LOCKMODE lockmode)

     937                 :            :

     940                 :            :

     942                 :            :         {
     943         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     944                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
     945                 :            :                                  errmsg("relation not found (OID %u)", relationId),
     946                 :            :                                  errdetail("This can be validly caused by a concurrent delete operation on this object.")));
     947                 :          0 :         }

     958                 :            : /* ----------------
     959                 :            :  *              try_relation_open - open any relation by relation OID
     960                 :            :  *
     961                 :            :  *              Same as relation_open, except return NULL instead of failing
     962                 :            :  *              if the relation does not exist.
     963                 :            :  * ----------------

     966                 :            : try_relation_open(Oid relationId, LOCKMODE lockmode, bool noWait)
     967                 :            : {

                 [ -  + ]
     971                 :            :

     974                 :            :         {

     977                 :            :                 else

     979                 :            :                         /*
     980                 :            :                          * noWait is a Greenplum addition to the open_relation code
     981                 :            :                          * basically to support INSERT ... FOR UPDATE NOWAIT.  Our NoWait
     982                 :            :                          * handling needs to be more tolerant of failed locks than standard
     983                 :            :                          * postgres largely due to the fact that we have to promote certain
     984                 :            :                          * update locks in order to handle distributed updates.
     985                 :            :                          */

     988                 :            :                 }

    1012                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
    1013                 :            :                                  errmsg("relation not found (OID %u)", relationId),
    1014                 :            :                                  errdetail("This can be validly caused by a concurrent delete operation on this object.")));
    1015                 :          0 :         }

    1027                 :            : 
    1028                 :            : /*
    1029                 :            :  * CdbTryOpenRelation -- Opens a relation with a specified lock mode.
    1030                 :            :  *
    1031                 :            :  * CDB: Like try_open_relation, except that it will upgrade the lock when needed
    1032                 :            :  * for distributed tables.
    1033                 :            :  */
    1034                 :            : Relation
    1035                 :            : CdbTryOpenRelation(Oid relid, LOCKMODE reqmode, bool noWait, bool *lockUpgraded)
    1036                 :            : {
    1037                 :     181806 :     LOCKMODE    lockmode = reqmode;
    1038                 :            :         Relation    rel;
    1039                 :            : 
    1040         [ +  + ]:     181806 :         if (lockUpgraded != NULL)
    1041                 :         16 :                 *lockUpgraded = false;
    1042                 :            : 
    1043                 :            :     /*
    1044                 :            :          * CDB: We need to upgrade the requested lock from RowExclusiveLock
    1045                 :            :          * to Exclusive lock (locking the whole relation) when doing updates
    1046                 :            :          * on distributed relations.
    1047                 :            :          *
    1048                 :            :          * Historically the need for this was do to limitations caused by lack of
    1049                 :            :          * distributed deadlock detection (which we now have) and due to our use
    1050                 :            :          * of logical mirroring (which has been replaced with physical replication).
    1051                 :            :          * However the need for this remains due to our current implementation
    1052                 :            :          * of partitioning.
    1053                 :            :          *
    1054                 :            :          * Specifically in the case of INSERT SELECT we do not know which partitions
    1055                 :            :          * we may be updating.  So we need to serialize UPDATE and DELETE operations
    1056                 :            :          * to the same partitioned table.  
    1057                 :            :          *
    1058                 :            :          * Note: This code could be improved substantiatally.
    1059                 :            :          */
    1060         [ +  + ]:     181806 :         if (lockmode == RowExclusiveLock)
    1061                 :            :         {
    1062                 :     103182 :                 rel = try_heap_open(relid, NoLock, noWait);
    1063         [ -  + ]:     103182 :                 if (!rel)
    1064                 :          0 :                         return NULL;
    1065                 :            :                 
    1066 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     103182 :                 if (rel->rd_cdbpolicy &&
    1067                 :     103124 :                         rel->rd_cdbpolicy->ptype == POLICYTYPE_PARTITIONED)
    1068                 :            :                 {
    1069                 :      26629 :                         lockmode = ExclusiveLock;
    1070         [ +  + ]:      26629 :                         if (lockUpgraded != NULL)
    1071                 :          4 :                                 *lockUpgraded = true;
    1072                 :      26629 :                 }
    1073                 :     103182 :                 relation_close(rel, NoLock);
    1074                 :     103182 :     }
    1075                 :            : 
    1076                 :     181806 :         rel = try_heap_open(relid, lockmode, noWait);
    1077         [ -  + ]:     181806 :         if (!RelationIsValid(rel))
    1078                 :          0 :                 return NULL;
    1079                 :            : 
    1080                 :            :         /* 
    1081                 :            :          * There is a slim chance that ALTER TABLE SET DISTRIBUTED BY may
    1082                 :            :          * have altered the distribution policy between the time that we
    1083                 :            :          * decided to upgrade the lock and the time we opened the relation
    1084                 :            :          * with the lock.  Double check that our chosen lock mode is still
    1085                 :            :          * okay.
    1086                 :            :          */
    1087 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:     181806 :         if (lockmode == RowExclusiveLock &&
    1088         [ +  + ]:      76553 :                 rel->rd_cdbpolicy &&
    1089                 :      76495 :                 rel->rd_cdbpolicy->ptype == POLICYTYPE_PARTITIONED)
    1090                 :            :         {
    1091                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "relation \"%s\" concurrently updated", 
    1092                 :            :                          RelationGetRelationName(rel));
    1093                 :          0 :         }
    1094                 :            : 
    1095                 :     181806 :         return rel;
    1096                 :     181806 : }                                       /* CdbOpenRelation */
    1097                 :            : 
    1098                 :            : /*
    1099                 :            :  * CdbOpenRelation -- Opens a relation with a specified lock mode.
    1100                 :            :  *
    1101                 :            :  * CDB: Like CdbTryOpenRelation, except that it guarantees either
    1102                 :            :  * an error or a valid opened relation returned.

    1111         [ -  + ]:     103107 :         if (!RelationIsValid(rel))
    1112                 :            :         {
    1113         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    1114                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
    1115                 :            :                                  errmsg("relation not found (OID %u)", relid),
    1116                 :            :                                  errdetail("This can be validly caused by a concurrent delete operation on this object.")));
    1117                 :          0 :         }

    1123                 :            : /*

    1125                 :            :  *

    1148                 :          0 :                 }

    1152                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
    1153                 :            :                                          errmsg("relation \"%s\" does not exist",
    1154                 :            :                                                         relation->relname)));
    1155                 :            :                 }
    1156                 :          0 :         }
    1157                 :            : 
    1158                 :      78699 :         return rel;

    1164                 :            : /* ----------------

    1192         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,

    1219                 :            : /* ----------------
    1220                 :            :  *              relation_openrv - open any relation specified by a RangeVar
    1221                 :            :  *
    1222                 :            :  *              Same as relation_open, but the relation is specified by a RangeVar.
    1223                 :            :  * ----------------
    1224                 :            :  */
    1225                 :            : Relation
    1226                 :            : relation_openrv(const RangeVar *relation, LOCKMODE lockmode)
    1227                 :            : {
    1228                 :            :         Oid                     relOid;
    1229                 :            :         Relation    rel;
    1230                 :            : 
    1231                 :            :         /*
    1232                 :            :          * Check for shared-cache-inval messages before trying to open the
    1233                 :            :          * relation.  This is needed to cover the case where the name identifies a
    1234                 :            :          * rel that has been dropped and recreated since the start of our
    1235                 :            :          * transaction: if we don't flush the old syscache entry then we'll latch
    1236                 :            :          * onto that entry and suffer an error when we do RelationIdGetRelation.
    1237                 :            :          * Note that relation_open does not need to do this, since a relation's
    1238                 :            :          * OID never changes.
    1239                 :            :          *
    1240                 :            :          * We skip this if asked for NoLock, on the assumption that the caller has
    1241                 :            :          * already ensured some appropriate lock is held.
    1242                 :            :          */
    1243         [ +  - ]:     104031 :         if (lockmode != NoLock)
    1244                 :     104031 :                 AcceptInvalidationMessages();
    1245                 :            : 
    1246                 :            :         /* Look up the appropriate relation using namespace search */
    1247                 :     103999 :         relOid = RangeVarGetRelid(relation, false);
    1248                 :            : 
    1249                 :            :         /* 
    1250                 :            :          * use try_relation_open instead of relation_open so that we can
    1251                 :            :          * throw a more graceful error message if the relation was dropped
    1252                 :            :          * between the RangeVarGetRelid and when we try to open the relation.
    1253                 :            :          */
    1254                 :     103999 :         rel = try_relation_open(relOid, lockmode, false);
    1255                 :            : 
    1256         [ -  + ]:     103999 :         if (!RelationIsValid(rel))
    1257                 :            :         {
    1258         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (relation->schemaname)
    1259                 :            :                 {
    1260         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    1261                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
    1262                 :            :                                          errmsg("relation \"%s.%s\" does not exist",
    1263                 :            :                                                         relation->schemaname, relation->relname)));
    1264                 :          0 :                 }
    1265                 :            :                 else
    1266                 :            :                 {
    1267         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    1268                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
    1269                 :            :                                          errmsg("relation \"%s\" does not exist",
    1270                 :            :                                                         relation->relname)));
    1271                 :            :                 }
    1272                 :          0 :         }
    1273                 :            : 
    1274                 :     103999 :         return rel;
    1275                 :            : }
    1276                 :            : 
    1277                 :            : /* ----------------
    1278                 :            :  *              try_relation_openrv - open any relation specified by a RangeVar
    1279                 :            :  *
    1280                 :            :  *              Same as relation_openrv, but return NULL instead of failing for
    1281                 :            :  *              relation-not-found.  (Note that some other causes, such as
    1282                 :            :  *              permissions problems, will still result in an ereport.)
    1283                 :            :  * ----------------
    1284                 :            :  */
    1285                 :            : Relation
    1286                 :            : try_relation_openrv(const RangeVar *relation, LOCKMODE lockmode, bool noWait)
    1287                 :            : {
    1288                 :            :         Oid                     relOid;
    1289                 :            : 
    1290                 :            :         /*
    1291                 :            :          * Check for shared-cache-inval messages before trying to open the
    1292                 :            :          * relation.  This is needed to cover the case where the name identifies a
    1293                 :            :          * rel that has been dropped and recreated since the start of our
    1294                 :            :          * transaction: if we don't flush the old syscache entry then we'll latch
    1295                 :            :          * onto that entry and suffer an error when we do RelationIdGetRelation.
    1296                 :            :          * Note that relation_open does not need to do this, since a relation's
    1297                 :            :          * OID never changes.
    1298                 :            :          *
    1299                 :            :          * We skip this if asked for NoLock, on the assumption that the caller has
    1300                 :            :          * already ensured some appropriate lock is held.
    1301                 :            :          */
    1302         [ +  - ]:         94 :         if (lockmode != NoLock)
    1303                 :         94 :                 AcceptInvalidationMessages();
    1304                 :            : 
    1305                 :            :         /* Look up the appropriate relation using namespace search */
    1306                 :         94 :         relOid = RangeVarGetRelid(relation, true);
    1307                 :            : 
    1308                 :            :         /* Return NULL on not-found */
    1309         [ +  + ]:         94 :         if (!OidIsValid(relOid))
    1310                 :          1 :                 return NULL;
    1311                 :            : 
    1312                 :            :         /* Let relation_open do the rest */
    1313                 :         93 :         return try_relation_open(relOid, lockmode, noWait);
    1314                 :         94 : }
    1315                 :            : 
    1316                 :            : /* ----------------
    1317                 :            :  *              relation_close - close any relation
    1318                 :            :  *
    1319                 :            :  *              If lockmode is not "NoLock", we then release the specified lock.
    1320                 :            :  *
    1321                 :            :  *              Note that it is often sensible to hold a lock beyond relation_close;
    1322                 :            :  *              in that case, the lock is released automatically at xact end.
    1323                 :            :  * ----------------
    1324                 :            :  */
    1325                 :            : void
    1326                 :            : relation_close(Relation relation, LOCKMODE lockmode)
    1327                 :            : {
    1328                 :   17701430 :         LockRelId       relid = relation->rd_lockInfo.lockRelId;
    1329                 :            : 
    1330 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:   17701430 :         Assert(lockmode >= NoLock && lockmode < MAX_LOCKMODES);
                 [ -  + ]
    1331                 :            : 
    1332                 :            :         /* The relcache does the real work... */
    1333                 :   17701430 :         RelationClose(relation);
    1334                 :            : 
    1335         [ +  + ]:   17701430 :         if (lockmode != NoLock)
    1336                 :   14271585 :                 UnlockRelationId(&relid, lockmode);
    1337                 :   17701430 : }
    1338                 :            : 
    1339                 :            : 
    1340                 :            : /* ----------------
    1341                 :            :  *              heap_open - open a heap relation by relation OID
    1342                 :            :  *
    1343                 :            :  *              This is essentially relation_open plus check that the relation
    1344                 :            :  *              is not an index nor a composite type.  (The caller should also
    1345                 :            :  *              check that it's not a view before assuming it has storage.)

    1358                 :            :                                  errmsg("\"%s\" is an index",
    1359                 :            :                                                 RelationGetRelationName(r))));
    1360         [ -  + ]:   15225100 :         else if (r->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE)
    1361         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,

    1363                 :            :                                  errmsg("\"%s\" is a composite type",

    1365                 :            : 
    1366                 :   15225100 :         return r;
    1367                 :            : }
    1368                 :            :

    1392                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),

    1428                 :            : /* ----------------
    1429                 :            :  *              heap_beginscan  - begin relation scan
    1430                 :            :  *
    1431                 :            :  * heap_beginscan_strat offers an extended API that lets the caller control
    1432                 :            :  * whether a nondefault buffer access strategy can be used, and whether
    1433                 :            :  * syncscan can be chosen (possibly resulting in the scan not starting from
    1434                 :            :  * block zero).  Both of these default to TRUE with plain heap_beginscan.
    1435                 :            :  *
    1436                 :            :  * heap_beginscan_bm is an alternative entry point for setting up a
    1437                 :            :  * HeapScanDesc for a bitmap heap scan.  Although that scan technology is
    1438                 :            :  * really quite unlike a standard seqscan, there is just enough commonality
    1439                 :            :  * to make it worth using the same data structure.
    1440                 :            :  * ----------------
    1441                 :            :  */
    1442                 :            : HeapScanDesc
    1443                 :            : heap_beginscan(Relation relation, Snapshot snapshot,
    1444                 :            :                            int nkeys, ScanKey key)
    1445                 :            : {
    1446                 :     312001 :         return heap_beginscan_internal(relation, snapshot, nkeys, key,
    1447                 :            :                                                                    true, true, false);
    1448                 :            : }
    1449                 :            : 
    1450                 :            : HeapScanDesc
    1451                 :            : heap_beginscan_strat(Relation relation, Snapshot snapshot,
    1452                 :            :                                          int nkeys, ScanKey key,
    1453                 :            :                                          bool allow_strat, bool allow_sync)
    1454                 :            : {
    1455                 :     133717 :         return heap_beginscan_internal(relation, snapshot, nkeys, key,
    1456                 :     133717 :                                                                    allow_strat, allow_sync, false);
    1457                 :            : }
    1458                 :            : 
    1459                 :            : HeapScanDesc
    1460                 :            : heap_beginscan_bm(Relation relation, Snapshot snapshot,
    1461                 :            :                                   int nkeys, ScanKey key)
    1462                 :            : {
    1463                 :       2881 :         return heap_beginscan_internal(relation, snapshot, nkeys, key,
    1464                 :            :                                                                    false, false, true);
    1465                 :            : }
    1466                 :            : 
    1467                 :            : static HeapScanDesc
    1468                 :            : heap_beginscan_internal(Relation relation, Snapshot snapshot,
    1469                 :            :                                                 int nkeys, ScanKey key,
    1470                 :            :                                                 bool allow_strat, bool allow_sync,
    1471                 :            :                                                 bool is_bitmapscan)
    1472                 :            : {
    1473                 :            :         HeapScanDesc scan;
    1474                 :            : 
    1475                 :            :         /*
    1476                 :            :          * increment relation ref count while scanning relation
    1477                 :            :          *
    1478                 :            :          * This is just to make really sure the relcache entry won't go away while
    1479                 :            :          * the scan has a pointer to it.  Caller should be holding the rel open
    1480                 :            :          * anyway, so this is redundant in all normal scenarios...
    1481                 :            :          */
    1482                 :     448599 :         RelationIncrementReferenceCount(relation);
    1483                 :            : 
    1484                 :            :         /*
    1485                 :            :          * allocate and initialize scan descriptor
    1486                 :            :          */
    1487                 :     448599 :         scan = (HeapScanDesc) palloc(sizeof(HeapScanDescData));
    1488                 :            : 
    1489                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_rd = relation;
    1490                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_snapshot = snapshot;
    1491                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_nkeys = nkeys;
    1492                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_bitmapscan = is_bitmapscan;
    1493                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_strategy = NULL;    /* set in initscan */
    1494                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_allow_strat = allow_strat;
    1495                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_allow_sync = allow_sync;
    1496                 :            : 
    1497                 :            :         /*
    1498                 :            :          * we can use page-at-a-time mode if it's an MVCC-safe snapshot
    1499                 :            :          */
    1500                 :     448599 :         scan->rs_pageatatime = IsMVCCSnapshot(snapshot);
    1501                 :            : 
    1502                 :            :         /*
    1503                 :            :          * we do this here instead of in initscan() because heap_rescan also calls
    1504                 :            :          * initscan() and we don't want to allocate memory again
    1505                 :            :          */
    1506         [ +  + ]:     448599 :         if (nkeys > 0)
    1507                 :     108736 :                 scan->rs_key = (ScanKey) palloc(sizeof(ScanKeyData) * nkeys);
    1508                 :            :         else
    1509                 :     339863 :                 scan->rs_key = NULL;
    1510                 :            : 
    1511                 :     448599 :         initscan(scan, key, false);
    1512                 :            : 
    1513                 :     448599 :         return scan;
    1514                 :            : }
    1515                 :            : 
    1516                 :            : /* ----------------
    1517                 :            :  *              heap_rescan             - restart a relation scan
    1518                 :            :  * ----------------
    1519                 :            :  */

    1521                 :            : heap_rescan(HeapScanDesc scan,
    1522                 :            :                         ScanKey key)

    1525                 :            :          * unpin scan buffers

    1530                 :            :         /*
    1531                 :            :          * reinitialize scan descriptor
    1532                 :            :          */

    1534                 :      25303 : }

    1536                 :            : /* ----------------

    1538                 :            :  *

    1544                 :            : heap_endscan(HeapScanDesc scan)
    1545                 :            : {

    1549                 :            :          * unpin scan buffers

                 [ +  - ]

    1564                 :            :

    1566                 :     447339 : }
    1567                 :            : 
    1568                 :            : /* ----------------
    1569                 :            :  *              heap_getnext    - retrieve next tuple in scan
    1570                 :            :  *
    1571                 :            :  *              Fix to work with index relations.
    1572                 :            :  *              We don't return the buffer anymore, but you can get it from the
    1573                 :            :  *              returned HeapTuple.
    1574                 :            :  * ----------------
    1575                 :            :  */
    1576                 :            : 
    1577                 :            : #ifdef HEAPDEBUGALL
    1578                 :            : #define HEAPDEBUG_1 \
    1579                 :            :         elog(DEBUG2, "heap_getnext([%s,nkeys=%d],dir=%d) called", \
    1580                 :            :                  RelationGetRelationName(scan->rs_rd), scan->rs_nkeys, (int) direction)
    1581                 :            : #define HEAPDEBUG_2 \
    1582                 :            :         elog(DEBUG2, "heap_getnext returning EOS")
    1583                 :            : #define HEAPDEBUG_3 \
    1584                 :            :         elog(DEBUG2, "heap_getnext returning tuple")
    1585                 :            : #else
    1586                 :            : #define HEAPDEBUG_1
    1587                 :            : #define HEAPDEBUG_2
    1588                 :            : #define HEAPDEBUG_3
    1589                 :            : #endif   /* !defined(HEAPDEBUGALL) */
    1590                 :            : 
    1591                 :            : 
    1592                 :            : HeapTuple
    1593                 :            : heap_getnext(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanDirection direction)
    1594                 :            : {
    1595                 :   45848532 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_DECLARE;
    1596                 :            : 
    1597                 :            :         /* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
    1598                 :            : 
    1599                 :   45848532 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_ENTER;
    1600                 :            : 
    1601                 :            :         HEAPDEBUG_1;                            /* heap_getnext( info ) */
    1602                 :            : 
    1603         [ +  + ]:   45848532 :         if (scan->rs_pageatatime)
    1604                 :      60106 :                 heapgettup_pagemode(scan, direction,
    1605                 :      60106 :                                                         scan->rs_nkeys, scan->rs_key);
    1606                 :            :         else
    1607                 :   45788426 :                 heapgettup(scan, direction, scan->rs_nkeys, scan->rs_key);
    1608                 :            : 
    1609         [ +  + ]:   45848532 :         if (scan->rs_ctup.t_data == NULL)
    1610                 :            :         {
    1611                 :            :                 HEAPDEBUG_2;                    /* heap_getnext returning EOS */
    1612                 :            : 
    1613 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     178468 :                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_EXIT;
                 [ -  + ]
    1614                 :            :                 
    1615                 :     178468 :                 return NULL;
    1616                 :            :         }
    1617                 :            : 
    1618                 :            :         /*
    1619                 :            :          * if we get here it means we have a new current scan tuple, so point to
    1620                 :            :          * the proper return buffer and return the tuple.
    1621                 :            :          */
    1622                 :            :         HEAPDEBUG_3;                            /* heap_getnext returning tuple */
    1623                 :            : 
    1624 [ +  - ][ +  + ]:   45670064 :         pgstat_count_heap_getnext(scan->rs_rd);
    1625                 :            :

                 [ -  + ]
    1627                 :            : 
    1628                 :   45670064 :         return &(scan->rs_ctup);
    1629                 :   45848532 : }

    1631                 :            : 
    1632                 :            : /*
    1633                 :            :  *  heap_getnextx  - fill up tdata[] up to tdatacnt
    1634                 :            :  *
    1635                 :            :  *  Take all tuples from a page, and cram as much as possible into tdata[].
    1636                 :            :  *  tdatacnt - IN: size of tdata[]. OUT: number of elements filled in tdata[].
    1637                 :            :  *  EOS      - OUT: non-zero if end of stream reached.
    1638                 :            :  */
    1639                 :            : void
    1640                 :            : heap_getnextx(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanDirection dir,
    1641                 :            :                           HeapTupleData tdata[], int *tdatacnt,
    1642                 :            :                           int *seen_EOS)
    1643                 :            : {
    1644                 :     985130 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_DECLARE;
    1645                 :            : 
    1646                 :     985130 :         int maxcnt = *tdatacnt;
    1647                 :     985130 :         int retcnt = 0;
    1648                 :     985130 :         HeapTuple t = NULL;

    1650                 :     985130 :         bool forward = ScanDirectionIsForward(dir);
    1651                 :            : 
    1652                 :     985130 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_ENTER;

    1654                 :            :         /*

    1656                 :            :          *    or 3. we have to qualify keys.
    1657                 :            :          */
    1658 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     985130 :         bool onebyone = (!backward && !forward) || !scan->rs_pageatatime || scan->rs_nkeys;
                 [ +  + ]
    1659                 :            : 
    1660         [ -  + ]:     985130 :         if (maxcnt <= 0)
    1661                 :            :         {
    1662         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    1663                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
    1664                 :            :                                  errmsg("illegal value in tdatacnt")));
    1665                 :          0 :         }
    1666                 :            : 
    1667         [ +  + ]:     985130 :         if (scan->rs_pageatatime)
    1668                 :     973758 :                 heapgettup_pagemode(scan, dir,
    1669                 :     973758 :                                                         scan->rs_nkeys, scan->rs_key);
    1670                 :            :         else

    1672                 :            : 
    1673 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     985130 :         if (NULL == (t = scan->rs_ctup.t_data ? &scan->rs_ctup : 0))
    1674                 :            :         {

    1676                 :            :                 
    1677 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     149821 :                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_EXIT;
                 [ -  + ]
    1678                 :            :                 
    1679                 :     149821 :                 return;
    1680                 :            :         }
    1681                 :            : 
    1682                 :     835309 :         tdata[retcnt++] = *t;
    1683 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:     835309 :         if (!onebyone && retcnt < maxcnt)
    1684                 :            :         {
    1685                 :     824208 :                 HeapTuple tuple = &(scan->rs_ctup);
    1686                 :            :                 int lineindex, linesleft;
    1687                 :     824208 :                 BlockNumber page = scan->rs_cblock;
    1688 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     824208 :                 Page dp = (Page) BufferGetPage(scan->rs_cbuf);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    1689                 :     824208 :                 int lines = scan->rs_ntuples;
    1690                 :            : 
    1691                 :            :                 /* go through all tuples in this page */
    1692         [ +  - ]:     824208 :                 if (forward)

    1694                 :     824208 :                         lineindex = scan->rs_cindex + 1;

    1696                 :     824208 :                 }
    1697                 :            :                 else
    1698                 :            :                 {
    1699                 :          0 :                         lineindex = scan->rs_cindex - 1;
    1700                 :          0 :                         linesleft = lineindex + 1;

    1702                 :            : 
    1703 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:  152946216 :                 while (linesleft > 0 && retcnt < maxcnt)

    1733                 :            : 
    1734                 :            : 
    1735                 :            : /*
    1736                 :            :  *      heap_fetch              - retrieve tuple with given tid
    1737                 :            :  *
    1738                 :            :  * On entry, tuple->t_self is the TID to fetch.  We pin the buffer holding
    1739                 :            :  * the tuple, fill in the remaining fields of *tuple, and check the tuple
    1740                 :            :  * against the specified snapshot.
    1741                 :            :  *
    1742                 :            :  * If successful (tuple found and passes snapshot time qual), then *userbuf
    1743                 :            :  * is set to the buffer holding the tuple and TRUE is returned.  The caller
    1744                 :            :  * must unpin the buffer when done with the tuple.
    1745                 :            :  *
    1746                 :            :  * If the tuple is not found (ie, item number references a deleted slot),
    1747                 :            :  * then tuple->t_data is set to NULL and FALSE is returned.
    1748                 :            :  *
    1749                 :            :  * If the tuple is found but fails the time qual check, then FALSE is returned
    1750                 :            :  * but tuple->t_data is left pointing to the tuple.
    1751                 :            :  *
    1752                 :            :  * keep_buf determines what is done with the buffer in the FALSE-result cases.
    1753                 :            :  * When the caller specifies keep_buf = true, we retain the pin on the buffer
    1754                 :            :  * and return it in *userbuf (so the caller must eventually unpin it); when
    1755                 :            :  * keep_buf = false, the pin is released and *userbuf is set to InvalidBuffer.
    1756                 :            :  *
    1757                 :            :  * stats_relation is the relation to charge the heap_fetch operation against
    1758                 :            :  * for statistical purposes.  (This could be the heap rel itself, an
    1759                 :            :  * associated index, or NULL to not count the fetch at all.)
    1760                 :            :  *
    1761                 :            :  * heap_fetch does not follow HOT chains: only the exact TID requested will
    1762                 :            :  * be fetched.
    1763                 :            :  *
    1764                 :            :  * It is somewhat inconsistent that we ereport() on invalid block number but
    1765                 :            :  * return false on invalid item number.  There are a couple of reasons though.
    1766                 :            :  * One is that the caller can relatively easily check the block number for
    1767                 :            :  * validity, but cannot check the item number without reading the page
    1768                 :            :  * himself.  Another is that when we are following a t_ctid link, we can be
    1769                 :            :  * reasonably confident that the page number is valid (since VACUUM shouldn't
    1770                 :            :  * truncate off the destination page without having killed the referencing
    1771                 :            :  * tuple first), but the item number might well not be good.
    1772                 :            :  */
    1773                 :            : bool
    1774                 :            : heap_fetch(Relation relation,
    1775                 :            :                    Snapshot snapshot,
    1776                 :            :                    HeapTuple tuple,
    1777                 :            :                    Buffer *userbuf,
    1778                 :            :                    bool keep_buf,
    1779                 :            :                    Relation stats_relation)
    1780                 :            : {
    1781                 :     707413 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_DECLARE;
    1782                 :            : 
    1783                 :            :         bool result;
    1784                 :            : 
    1785                 :     707413 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_ENTER;
    1786                 :            : 
    1787                 :            :         /* Assume *userbuf is undefined on entry */
    1788                 :     707413 :         *userbuf = InvalidBuffer;
    1789                 :     707413 :         result = heap_release_fetch(relation, snapshot, tuple,
    1790                 :     707413 :                                                           userbuf, keep_buf, stats_relation);
    1791                 :            : 
    1792                 :            : 
    1793 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     707413 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_EXIT;
                 [ -  + ]
    1794                 :            : 
    1795                 :     707413 :         return result;
    1796                 :            : }
    1797                 :            : 
    1798                 :            : /*
    1799                 :            :  *      heap_release_fetch              - retrieve tuple with given tid
    1800                 :            :  *
    1801                 :            :  * This has the same API as heap_fetch except that if *userbuf is not
    1802                 :            :  * InvalidBuffer on entry, that buffer will be released before reading
    1803                 :            :  * the new page.  This saves a separate ReleaseBuffer step and hence
    1804                 :            :  * one entry into the bufmgr when looping through multiple fetches.
    1805                 :            :  * Also, if *userbuf is the same buffer that holds the target tuple,
    1806                 :            :  * we avoid bufmgr manipulation altogether.
    1807                 :            :  */
    1808                 :            : bool
    1809                 :            : heap_release_fetch(Relation relation,
    1810                 :            :                                    Snapshot snapshot,
    1811                 :            :                                    HeapTuple tuple,
    1812                 :            :                                    Buffer *userbuf,
    1813                 :            :                                    bool keep_buf,
    1814                 :            :                                    Relation stats_relation)
    1815                 :            : {
    1816                 :     707413 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
    1817                 :            : 
    1818                 :     707413 :         ItemPointer tid = &(tuple->t_self);
    1819                 :            :         ItemId          lp;
    1820                 :            :         Buffer          buffer;
    1821                 :            :         PageHeader      dp;
    1822                 :            :         OffsetNumber offnum;
    1823                 :            :         bool            valid;
    1824                 :            : 
    1825                 :            :         /*
    1826                 :            :          * get the buffer from the relation descriptor. Note that this does a
    1827                 :            :          * buffer pin, and releases the old *userbuf if not InvalidBuffer.
    1828                 :            :          */
    1829                 :            :         
    1830                 :            :         // -------- MirroredLock ----------
    1831 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:     707413 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_LOCK;
         [ #  # ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    1832                 :            :         
    1833                 :     707413 :         buffer = ReleaseAndReadBuffer(*userbuf, relation,
    1834 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     707413 :                                                                   ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid));
         [ -  + ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    1835                 :            : 
    1836                 :            :         /*
    1837                 :            :          * Need share lock on buffer to examine tuple commit status.
    1838                 :            :          */
    1839                 :     707413 :         LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
    1840 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     707413 :         dp = (PageHeader) BufferGetPage(buffer);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    1841                 :            : 
    1842                 :            :         /*

    1845                 :            :          */

                 [ -  + ]
    1847 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     707413 :         if (offnum < FirstOffsetNumber || offnum > PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(dp))
                 [ -  + ]

    1849                 :          0 :                 LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
    1850                 :            :                 
    1851 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_UNLOCK;
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
                 [ #  # ]
    1852                 :            :                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
    1853                 :            :                 
    1854         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (keep_buf)
    1855                 :          0 :                         *userbuf = buffer;
    1856                 :            :                 else
    1857                 :            :                 {
    1858                 :          0 :                         ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
    1859                 :          0 :                         *userbuf = InvalidBuffer;
    1860                 :            :                 }
    1861                 :          0 :                 tuple->t_data = NULL;
    1862                 :          0 :                 return false;
    1863                 :            :         }
    1864                 :            : 
    1865                 :            :         /*
    1866                 :            :          * get the item line pointer corresponding to the requested tid
    1867                 :            :          */

    1869                 :            : 
    1870                 :            :         /*

         [ #  # ][ #  # ]

                 [ #  # ]

    1934                 :            : /*
    1935                 :            :  *      heap_hot_search_buffer  - search HOT chain for tuple satisfying snapshot
    1936                 :            :  *
    1937                 :            :  * On entry, *tid is the TID of a tuple (either a simple tuple, or the root
    1938                 :            :  * of a HOT chain), and buffer is the buffer holding this tuple.  We search
    1939                 :            :  * for the first chain member satisfying the given snapshot.  If one is
    1940                 :            :  * found, we update *tid to reference that tuple's offset number, and
    1941                 :            :  * return TRUE.  If no match, return FALSE without modifying *tid.
    1942                 :            :  *
    1943                 :            :  * If all_dead is not NULL, we check non-visible tuples to see if they are
    1944                 :            :  * globally dead; *all_dead is set TRUE if all members of the HOT chain
    1945                 :            :  * are vacuumable, FALSE if not.
    1946                 :            :  *
    1947                 :            :  * Unlike heap_fetch, the caller must already have pin and (at least) share
    1948                 :            :  * lock on the buffer; it is still pinned/locked at exit.  Also unlike
    1949                 :            :  * heap_fetch, we do not report any pgstats count; caller may do so if wanted.
    1950                 :            :  */
    1951                 :            : bool
    1952                 :            : heap_hot_search_buffer(Relation rel, ItemPointer tid, Buffer buffer, Snapshot snapshot,
    1953                 :            :                                            bool *all_dead)

    1955 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:    2071767 :         Page            dp = (Page) BufferGetPage(buffer);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]

    1957                 :            :         OffsetNumber offnum;
    1958                 :            :         bool            at_chain_start;
    1959                 :            : 
    1960         [ +  + ]:    2071767 :         if (all_dead)
    1961                 :     263745 :                 *all_dead = true;
    1962                 :            : 
    1963 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:    2071767 :         Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(RecentGlobalXmin));
    1964                 :            : 
    1965 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:    2071767 :         Assert(ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid) == BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer));
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    1966 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:    2071767 :         offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(tid);
                 [ -  + ]
    1967                 :    2071767 :         at_chain_start = true;
    1968                 :            : 
    1969                 :            :         /* Scan through possible multiple members of HOT-chain */

    1971                 :            :         {
    1972                 :            :                 ItemId          lp;

    1974                 :            : 
    1975                 :            :                 /* check for bogus TID */

                 [ -  + ]

    1978                 :            :

    1980                 :            :

    1983                 :            :                 {

    2052                 :            : /*
    2053                 :            :  *      heap_hot_search         - search HOT chain for tuple satisfying snapshot
    2054                 :            :  *
    2055                 :            :  * This has the same API as heap_hot_search_buffer, except that the caller
    2056                 :            :  * does not provide the buffer containing the page, rather we access it
    2057                 :            :  * locally.
    2058                 :            :  */

    2064                 :            :         Buffer          buffer;
    2065                 :            :

         [ -  + ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    2067                 :     263760 :         LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
    2068                 :     263760 :         result = heap_hot_search_buffer(relation, tid, buffer, snapshot, all_dead);
    2069                 :     263760 :         LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
    2070                 :     263760 :         ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
    2071                 :     263760 :         return result;
    2072                 :            : }
    2073                 :            : 
    2074                 :            : /*
    2075                 :            :  *      heap_get_latest_tid -  get the latest tid of a specified tuple
    2076                 :            :  *
    2077                 :            :  * Actually, this gets the latest version that is visible according to
    2078                 :            :  * the passed snapshot.  You can pass SnapshotDirty to get the very latest,
    2079                 :            :  * possibly uncommitted version.
    2080                 :            :  *
    2081                 :            :  * *tid is both an input and an output parameter: it is updated to
    2082                 :            :  * show the latest version of the row.  Note that it will not be changed
    2083                 :            :  * if no version of the row passes the snapshot test.
    2084                 :            :  */
    2085                 :            : void
    2086                 :            : heap_get_latest_tid(Relation relation,
    2087                 :            :                                         Snapshot snapshot,
    2088                 :            :                                         ItemPointer tid)

    2090                 :         34 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
    2091                 :            :

    2093                 :            :         ItemPointerData ctid;
    2094                 :            :         TransactionId priorXmax;
    2095                 :            : 
    2096                 :            :         /* this is to avoid Assert failures on bad input */
    2097 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         34 :         if (!ItemPointerIsValid(tid))
    2098                 :          0 :                 return;
    2099                 :            : 
    2100                 :            :         /*
    2101                 :            :          * Since this can be called with user-supplied TID, don't trust the input
    2102                 :            :          * too much.  (RelationGetNumberOfBlocks is an expensive check, so we
    2103                 :            :          * don't check t_ctid links again this way.  Note that it would not do to
    2104                 :            :          * call it just once and save the result, either.)
    2105                 :            :          */
    2106 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:         34 :         blk = ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid);
         [ -  + ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    2107         [ -  + ]:         34 :         if (blk >= RelationGetNumberOfBlocks(relation))
    2108                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "block number %u is out of range for relation \"%s\"",
    2109                 :            :                          blk, RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    2110                 :            : 
    2111                 :            :         /*
    2112                 :            :          * Loop to chase down t_ctid links.  At top of loop, ctid is the tuple we
    2113                 :            :          * need to examine, and *tid is the TID we will return if ctid turns out
    2114                 :            :          * to be bogus.
    2115                 :            :          *
    2116                 :            :          * Note that we will loop until we reach the end of the t_ctid chain.
    2117                 :            :          * Depending on the snapshot passed, there might be at most one visible
    2118                 :            :          * version of the row, but we don't try to optimize for that.
    2119                 :            :          */
    2120                 :         34 :         ctid = *tid;
    2121                 :         34 :         priorXmax = InvalidTransactionId;       /* cannot check first XMIN */
    2122                 :         34 :         for (;;)
    2123                 :            :         {
    2124                 :            :                 Buffer          buffer;
    2125                 :            :                 PageHeader      dp;
    2126                 :            :                 OffsetNumber offnum;
    2127                 :            :                 ItemId          lp;
    2128                 :            :                 HeapTupleData tp;
    2129                 :            :                 bool            valid;
    2130                 :            :

    2132                 :            :                  * Read, pin, and lock the page.
    2133                 :            :                  */
    2134                 :            : 
    2135                 :            :                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
    2136 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:         43 :                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_LOCK;
         [ #  # ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    2137                 :            : 
    2138 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         43 :                 buffer = ReadBuffer(relation, ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&ctid));

    2141                 :            :

    2146                 :            :                  */

    2148 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         43 :                 if (offnum < FirstOffsetNumber || offnum > PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(dp))
                 [ -  + ]
