    2221                 :            : /*
    2222                 :            :  * UpdateXmaxHintBits - update tuple hint bits after xmax transaction ends
    2223                 :            :  *
    2224                 :            :  * This is called after we have waited for the XMAX transaction to terminate.
    2225                 :            :  * If the transaction aborted, we guarantee the XMAX_INVALID hint bit will
    2226                 :            :  * be set on exit.      If the transaction committed, we set the XMAX_COMMITTED
    2227                 :            :  * hint bit if possible --- but beware that that may not yet be possible,
    2228                 :            :  * if the transaction committed asynchronously.  Hence callers should look
    2229                 :            :  * only at XMAX_INVALID.
    2230                 :            :  */
    2231                 :            : static void
    2232                 :            : UpdateXmaxHintBits(HeapTupleHeader tuple, Buffer buffer, TransactionId xid, Relation rel)
    2233                 :            : {
    2234 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(TransactionIdEquals(HeapTupleHeaderGetXmax(tuple), xid));
    2235                 :            : 
    2236         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!(tuple->t_infomask & (HEAP_XMAX_COMMITTED | HEAP_XMAX_INVALID)))
    2237                 :            :         {
    2238         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (TransactionIdDidCommit(xid))
    2239                 :          0 :                         HeapTupleSetHintBits(tuple, buffer, rel, HEAP_XMAX_COMMITTED,
    2240                 :          0 :                                                                  xid);
    2241                 :            :                 else
    2242                 :          0 :                         HeapTupleSetHintBits(tuple, buffer, rel, HEAP_XMAX_INVALID,
    2243                 :            :                                                                  InvalidTransactionId);
    2244                 :          0 :         }
    2245                 :          0 : }
    2246                 :            : 
    2247                 :            : /*
    2248                 :            :  *      heap_insert             - insert tuple into a heap
    2249                 :            :  *
    2250                 :            :  * The new tuple is stamped with current transaction ID and the specified
    2251                 :            :  * command ID.
    2252                 :            :  *
    2253                 :            :  * If use_wal is false, the new tuple is not logged in WAL, even for a
    2254                 :            :  * non-temp relation.  Safe usage of this behavior requires that we arrange
    2255                 :            :  * that all new tuples go into new pages not containing any tuples from other
    2256                 :            :  * transactions, and that the relation gets fsync'd before commit.
    2257                 :            :  * (See also heap_sync() comments)
    2258                 :            :  *
    2259                 :            :  * use_fsm is passed directly to RelationGetBufferForTuple, which see for
    2260                 :            :  * more info.
    2261                 :            :  *
    2262                 :            :  * Note that use_wal and use_fsm will be applied when inserting into the
    2263                 :            :  * heap's TOAST table, too, if the tuple requires any out-of-line data.
    2264                 :            :  *
    2265                 :            :  * The return value is the OID assigned to the tuple (either here or by the
    2266                 :            :  * caller), or InvalidOid if no OID.  The header fields of *tup are updated
    2267                 :            :  * to match the stored tuple; in particular tup->t_self receives the actual
    2268                 :            :  * TID where the tuple was stored.      But note that any toasting of fields
    2269                 :            :  * within the tuple data is NOT reflected into *tup.
    2270                 :            :  */
    2271                 :            : Oid
    2272                 :            : heap_insert(Relation relation, HeapTuple tup, CommandId cid,
    2273                 :            :                         bool use_wal, bool use_fsm, TransactionId xid)
    2274                 :            : {
    2275                 :   32581349 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
    2276                 :            : 
    2277                 :   32581349 :         bool            isFrozen = (xid == FrozenTransactionId);

    2279                 :            :         Buffer          buffer;

    2281         [ -  + ]:   32581349 :         Insist(RelationIsHeap(relation));

    2293                 :            :                 /*

    2295                 :            :                  * caller.      There are a couple of ways this can happen.  At initial db
    2296                 :            :                  * creation, the backend program sets oids for tuples. When we define
    2297                 :            :                  * an index, we set the oid.  Finally, in the future, we may allow
    2298                 :            :                  * users to set their own object ids in order to support a persistent
    2299                 :            :                  * object store (objects need to contain pointers to one another).
    2300                 :            :                  */

    2482                 :            :  *

    2547                 :            :                                            FrozenTransactionId);
    2548                 :            : 
    2549 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_EXIT;
                 [ #  # ]
    2550                 :            : 
    2551                 :          0 :         return result;

    2554                 :            : /*
    2555                 :            :  * heap_trace_current_tuple
    2556                 :            :  *
    2557                 :            :  * Log a line showing the current tuple MVCC information.
    2558                 :            :  */
    2559                 :            : static void
    2560                 :            : heap_trace_current_tuple(
    2561                 :            :         char            *caller,
    2562                 :            : 
    2563                 :            :         HeapTuple       tuple)
    2564                 :            : {
    2565                 :            :         TupleVisibilitySummary tupleVisibilitySummary;
    2566                 :            :         char *summary;

    2568                 :          0 :         GetTupleVisibilitySummary(
    2569                 :          0 :                                                         tuple,
    2570                 :            :                                                         &tupleVisibilitySummary);
    2571                 :          0 :         summary = GetTupleVisibilitySummaryString(&tupleVisibilitySummary);
    2572                 :            : 
    2573                 :          0 :         elog(LOG, "Current tuple for %s: %s",
    2574                 :            :                  caller,
    2575                 :            :                  summary);
    2576                 :            : 
    2577                 :          0 :         pfree(summary);
    2578                 :          0 : }
    2579                 :            : 
    2580                 :            : /*
    2581                 :            :  *      heap_delete - delete a tuple
    2582                 :            :  *
    2583                 :            :  * NB: do not call this directly unless you are prepared to deal with
    2584                 :            :  * concurrent-update conditions.  Use simple_heap_delete instead.
    2585                 :            :  *
    2586                 :            :  *      relation - table to be modified (caller must hold suitable lock)
    2587                 :            :  *      tid - TID of tuple to be deleted
    2588                 :            :  *      ctid - output parameter, used only for failure case (see below)
    2589                 :            :  *      update_xmax - output parameter, used only for failure case (see below)
    2590                 :            :  *      cid - delete command ID (used for visibility test, and stored into
    2591                 :            :  *              cmax if successful)
    2592                 :            :  *      crosscheck - if not InvalidSnapshot, also check tuple against this
    2593                 :            :  *      wait - true if should wait for any conflicting update to commit/abort
    2594                 :            :  *
    2595                 :            :  * Normal, successful return value is HeapTupleMayBeUpdated, which
    2596                 :            :  * actually means we did delete it.  Failure return codes are
    2597                 :            :  * HeapTupleSelfUpdated, HeapTupleUpdated, or HeapTupleBeingUpdated
    2598                 :            :  * (the last only possible if wait == false).
    2599                 :            :  *
    2600                 :            :  * In the failure cases, the routine returns the tuple's t_ctid and t_xmax.
    2601                 :            :  * If t_ctid is the same as tid, the tuple was deleted; if different, the
    2602                 :            :  * tuple was updated, and t_ctid is the location of the replacement tuple.
    2603                 :            :  * (t_xmax is needed to verify that the replacement tuple matches.)
    2604                 :            :  */
    2605                 :            : HTSU_Result
    2606                 :            : heap_delete(Relation relation, ItemPointer tid,
    2607                 :            :                         ItemPointer ctid, TransactionId *update_xmax,
    2608                 :            :                         CommandId cid, Snapshot crosscheck, bool wait)
    2609                 :            : {
    2610                 :     454136 :         return heap_delete_xid(relation, tid,
    2611                 :     454136 :                                                    GetCurrentTransactionId(), ctid,
    2612                 :     454136 :                                                    update_xmax, cid, crosscheck, wait);
    2613                 :            : }
    2614                 :            : 
    2615                 :            : static HTSU_Result

    2677                 :            :                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------

    2693                 :            : 
    2694                 :            :                 /*

         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
                 [ #  # ]
    2707                 :            :

    2709                 :            :

    2725                 :            :                          * essentially a situation of upgrading our former shared lock to

    2729                 :          0 :                 }

    2732                 :            :                         /* wait for regular transaction to end */

    2734                 :            :                         
    2735                 :            :                         // -------- MirroredLock ----------
    2736 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_LOCK;
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
                 [ #  # ]
    2737                 :            :                         
    2738                 :          0 :                         LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
    2739                 :            : 
    2740                 :            :                         /*

    2744                 :            :                          */

    2747                 :            :                                                                          xwait))

    2885                 :            : /*
    2886                 :            :  *      simple_heap_delete - delete a tuple
    2887                 :            :  *
    2888                 :            :  * This routine may be used to delete a tuple when concurrent updates of
    2889                 :            :  * the target tuple are not expected (for example, because we have a lock
    2890                 :            :  * on the relation associated with the tuple).  Any failure is reported
    2891                 :            :  * via ereport().
    2892                 :            :  */
    2893                 :            : void
    2894                 :            : simple_heap_delete(Relation relation, ItemPointer tid)

    2896                 :    1496786 :         simple_heap_delete_xid(relation, tid, GetCurrentTransactionId());
    2897                 :    1496786 : }
    2898                 :            : 
    2899                 :            : void
    2900                 :            : simple_heap_delete_xid(Relation relation, ItemPointer tid, TransactionId xid)
    2901                 :            : {
    2902                 :            : 
    2903                 :    1627102 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_DECLARE;

    2907                 :            :         TransactionId update_xmax;
    2908                 :            : 
    2909                 :    1627102 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_ENTER;
    2910                 :            : 
    2911                 :    1627102 :         result = heap_delete_xid(relation, tid, xid,
    2912                 :            :                                                  &update_ctid, &update_xmax,
    2913                 :    1627102 :                                                  GetCurrentCommandId(true), InvalidSnapshot,
    2914                 :            :                                                  true /* wait for commit */ );
    2915   [ -  +  -  - ]:    1627102 :         switch (result)
    2916                 :            :         {
    2917                 :            :                 case HeapTupleSelfUpdated:
    2918                 :            :                         /* Tuple was already updated in current command? */
    2919                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "tuple already updated by self");
    2920                 :          0 :                         break;
    2921                 :            : 
    2922                 :            :                 case HeapTupleMayBeUpdated:
    2923                 :            :                         /* done successfully */
    2924                 :    1627102 :                         break;
    2925                 :            : 
    2926                 :            :                 case HeapTupleUpdated:
    2927                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "tuple concurrently updated");
    2928                 :          0 :                         break;
    2929                 :            : 
    2930                 :            :                 default:
    2931                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "unrecognized heap_delete status: %u", result);
    2932                 :          0 :                         break;
    2933                 :            :         }
    2934                 :            :

                 [ -  + ]

    2938                 :            : 
    2939                 :            : /*
    2940                 :            :  *      heap_update - replace a tuple
    2941                 :            :  *
    2942                 :            :  * NB: do not call this directly unless you are prepared to deal with
    2943                 :            :  * concurrent-update conditions.  Use simple_heap_update instead.
    2944                 :            :  *
    2945                 :            :  *      relation - table to be modified (caller must hold suitable lock)
    2946                 :            :  *      otid - TID of old tuple to be replaced
    2947                 :            :  *      newtup - newly constructed tuple data to store
    2948                 :            :  *      ctid - output parameter, used only for failure case (see below)
    2949                 :            :  *      update_xmax - output parameter, used only for failure case (see below)
    2950                 :            :  *      cid - update command ID (used for visibility test, and stored into
    2951                 :            :  *              cmax/cmin if successful)
    2952                 :            :  *      crosscheck - if not InvalidSnapshot, also check old tuple against this
    2953                 :            :  *      wait - true if should wait for any conflicting update to commit/abort
    2954                 :            :  *
    2955                 :            :  * Normal, successful return value is HeapTupleMayBeUpdated, which
    2956                 :            :  * actually means we *did* update it.  Failure return codes are
    2957                 :            :  * HeapTupleSelfUpdated, HeapTupleUpdated, or HeapTupleBeingUpdated
    2958                 :            :  * (the last only possible if wait == false).
    2959                 :            :  *
    2960                 :            :  * On success, the header fields of *newtup are updated to match the new
    2961                 :            :  * stored tuple; in particular, newtup->t_self is set to the TID where the
    2962                 :            :  * new tuple was inserted, and its HEAP_ONLY_TUPLE flag is set iff a HOT
    2963                 :            :  * update was done.  However, any TOAST changes in the new tuple's
    2964                 :            :  * data are not reflected into *newtup.
    2965                 :            :  *
    2966                 :            :  * In the failure cases, the routine returns the tuple's t_ctid and t_xmax.
    2967                 :            :  * If t_ctid is the same as otid, the tuple was deleted; if different, the
    2968                 :            :  * tuple was updated, and t_ctid is the location of the replacement tuple.
    2969                 :            :  * (t_xmax is needed to verify that the replacement tuple matches.)
    2970                 :            :  */

    3273                 :            :                 /*

    3276                 :            :                  * we weren't looking.  We have to recheck the available space after

    3278                 :            :                  * former amount of free space is still not enough; it's unlikely

    3282                 :            :                  * buffer locks on both old and new pages.      To avoid deadlock against
    3283                 :            :                  * some other backend trying to get the same two locks in the other
    3284                 :            :                  * order, we must be consistent about the order we get the locks in.
    3285                 :            :                  * We use the rule "lock the lower-numbered page of the relation
    3286                 :            :                  * first".  To implement this, we must do RelationGetBufferForTuple

    3331                 :            : 
    3332                 :            :         /*
    3333                 :            :          * At this point newbuf and buffer are both pinned and locked, and newbuf
    3334                 :            :          * has enough space for the new tuple.  If they are the same buffer, only
    3335                 :            :          * one pin is held.
    3336                 :            :          */
    3337                 :            : 
    3338         [ +  + ]:     402802 :         if (newbuf == buffer)
    3339                 :            :         {
    3340                 :            :                 /*
    3341                 :            :                  * Since the new tuple is going into the same page, we might be able
    3342                 :            :                  * to do a HOT update.  Check if any of the index columns have been
    3343                 :            :                  * changed.  If not, then HOT update is possible.
    3344                 :            :                  */
    3345         [ +  + ]:     327931 :                 if (HeapSatisfiesHOTUpdate(relation, hot_attrs, &oldtup, heaptup))
    3346                 :     317897 :                         use_hot_update = true;
    3347                 :     327931 :         }
    3348                 :            :         else
    3349                 :            :         {
    3350                 :            :                 /* Set a hint that the old page could use prune/defrag */
    3351                 :      74871 :                 PageSetFull(dp);
    3352                 :            :         }
    3353                 :            : 
    3354                 :            :         /* NO EREPORT(ERROR) from here till changes are logged */
    3355         [ -  + ]:     402802 :         START_CRIT_SECTION();
    3356                 :            : 
    3357                 :            :         /*
    3358                 :            :          * If this transaction commits, the old tuple will become DEAD sooner or
    3359                 :            :          * later.  Set flag that this page is a candidate for pruning once our xid
    3360                 :            :          * falls below the OldestXmin horizon.  If the transaction finally aborts,
    3361                 :            :          * the subsequent page pruning will be a no-op and the hint will be
    3362                 :            :          * cleared.
    3363                 :            :          *
    3364                 :            :          * XXX Should we set hint on newbuf as well?  If the transaction aborts,
    3365                 :            :          * there would be a prunable tuple in the newbuf; but for now we choose
    3366                 :            :          * not to optimize for aborts.  Note that heap_xlog_update must be kept in
    3367                 :            :          * sync if this decision changes.
    3368                 :            :          */
    3369 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     402802 :         PageSetPrunable(dp, xid);
         [ #  # ][ +  + ]
                 [ +  + ]
    3370                 :            : 
    3371         [ +  + ]:     402802 :         if (use_hot_update)
    3372                 :            :         {
    3373                 :            :                 /* Mark the old tuple as HOT-updated */
    3374                 :     317897 :                 HeapTupleSetHotUpdated(&oldtup);
    3375                 :            :                 /* And mark the new tuple as heap-only */
    3376                 :     317897 :                 HeapTupleSetHeapOnly(heaptup);
    3377                 :            :                 /* Mark the caller's copy too, in case different from heaptup */
    3378                 :     317897 :                 HeapTupleSetHeapOnly(newtup);
    3379                 :     317897 :         }
    3380                 :            :         else
    3381                 :            :         {
    3382                 :            :                 /* Make sure tuples are correctly marked as not-HOT */
    3383                 :      84905 :                 HeapTupleClearHotUpdated(&oldtup);
    3384                 :      84905 :                 HeapTupleClearHeapOnly(heaptup);
    3385                 :      84905 :                 HeapTupleClearHeapOnly(newtup);
    3386                 :            :         }
    3387                 :            : 
    3388                 :     402802 :         RelationPutHeapTuple(relation, newbuf, heaptup);        /* insert new tuple */
    3389                 :            :

    3392                 :            :                 /* Clear obsolete visibility flags ... */

    3394                 :            :                                                                            HEAP_XMAX_INVALID |
    3395                 :            :                                                                            HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI |
    3396                 :            :                                                                            HEAP_IS_LOCKED |
    3397                 :            :                                                                            HEAP_MOVED);
    3398                 :            :                 /* ... and store info about transaction updating this tuple */

    3400 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     327923 :                 HeapTupleHeaderSetCmax(oldtup.t_data, cid, iscombo);
                 [ +  + ]
    3401                 :     327923 :         }
    3402                 :            : 
    3403                 :            :         /* record address of new tuple in t_ctid of old one */

    3414                 :     402781 :                                                                                          newbuf, heaptup, false);

         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    3420                 :      74871 :                 }
    3421 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     402781 :                 PageSetLSN(BufferGetPage(buffer), recptr);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    3422 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     402781 :                 PageSetTLI(BufferGetPage(buffer), ThisTimeLineID);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    3423                 :     402781 :         }
    3424                 :            :

    3432                 :            :         // -------- MirroredLock ----------
    3433                 :            :         
    3434                 :            :         /*

    3497                 :            : /*
    3498                 :            :  * Check if the specified attribute's value is same in both given tuples.
    3499                 :            :  * Subroutine for HeapSatisfiesHOTUpdate.
    3500                 :            :  */
    3501                 :            : static bool
    3502                 :            : heap_tuple_attr_equals(TupleDesc tupdesc, int attrnum,
    3503                 :            :                                            HeapTuple tup1, HeapTuple tup2)
    3504                 :            : {
    3505                 :            :         Datum           value1,
    3506                 :            :                                 value2;
    3507                 :            :         bool            isnull1,
    3508                 :            :                                 isnull2;
    3509                 :            :         Form_pg_attribute att;
    3510                 :            : 
    3511                 :            :         /*
    3512                 :            :          * If it's a whole-tuple reference, say "not equal".  It's not really
    3513                 :            :          * worth supporting this case, since it could only succeed after a no-op
    3514                 :            :          * update, which is hardly a case worth optimizing for.
    3515                 :            :          */
    3516         [ -  + ]:     815301 :         if (attrnum == 0)
    3517                 :          0 :                 return false;
    3518                 :            : 
    3519                 :            :         /*
    3520                 :            :          * Likewise, automatically say "not equal" for any system attribute other
    3521                 :            :          * than OID and tableOID; we cannot expect these to be consistent in a HOT
    3522                 :            :          * chain, or even to be set correctly yet in the new tuple.
    3523                 :            :          */
    3524         [ +  + ]:     815301 :         if (attrnum < 0)
    3525                 :            :         {
    3526 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:     100505 :                 if (attrnum != ObjectIdAttributeNumber &&
    3527                 :          0 :                         attrnum != TableOidAttributeNumber)
    3528                 :          0 :                         return false;
    3529                 :     100505 :         }
    3530                 :            : 
    3531                 :            :         /*
    3532                 :            :          * Extract the corresponding values.  XXX this is pretty inefficient if
    3533                 :            :          * there are many indexed columns.      Should HeapSatisfiesHOTUpdate do a
    3534                 :            :          * single heap_deform_tuple call on each tuple, instead?  But that doesn't
    3535                 :            :          * work for system columns ...
    3536                 :            :          */
    3537                 :     815301 :         value1 = heap_getattr(tup1, attrnum, tupdesc, &isnull1);
    3538                 :     815301 :         value2 = heap_getattr(tup2, attrnum, tupdesc, &isnull2);
    3539                 :            : 
    3540                 :            :         /*
    3541                 :            :          * If one value is NULL and other is not, then they are certainly not
    3542                 :            :          * equal
    3543                 :            :          */
    3544         [ -  + ]:     815301 :         if (isnull1 != isnull2)
    3545                 :          0 :                 return false;
    3546                 :            : 
    3547                 :            :         /*
    3548                 :            :          * If both are NULL, they can be considered equal.
    3549                 :            :          */
    3550         [ -  + ]:     815301 :         if (isnull1)
    3551                 :          0 :                 return true;
    3552                 :            : 
    3553                 :            :         /*
    3554                 :            :          * We do simple binary comparison of the two datums.  This may be overly
    3555                 :            :          * strict because there can be multiple binary representations for the
    3556                 :            :          * same logical value.  But we should be OK as long as there are no false
    3557                 :            :          * positives.  Using a type-specific equality operator is messy because
    3558                 :            :          * there could be multiple notions of equality in different operator
    3559                 :            :          * classes; furthermore, we cannot safely invoke user-defined functions
    3560                 :            :          * while holding exclusive buffer lock.
    3561                 :            :          */
    3562         [ +  + ]:     815301 :         if (attrnum <= 0)
    3563                 :            :         {
    3564                 :            :                 /* The only allowed system columns are OIDs, so do this */
    3565                 :     100505 :                 return (DatumGetObjectId(value1) == DatumGetObjectId(value2));
    3566                 :            :         }
    3567                 :            :         else
    3568                 :            :         {
    3569 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     714796 :                 Assert(attrnum <= tupdesc->natts);
    3570                 :     714796 :                 att = tupdesc->attrs[attrnum - 1];
    3571                 :     714796 :                 return datumIsEqual(value1, value2, att->attbyval, att->attlen);
    3572                 :            :         }
    3573                 :     815301 : }
    3574                 :            : 
    3575                 :            : /*
    3576                 :            :  * Check if the old and new tuples represent a HOT-safe update. To be able
    3577                 :            :  * to do a HOT update, we must not have changed any columns used in index
    3578                 :            :  * definitions.
    3579                 :            :  *
    3580                 :            :  * The set of attributes to be checked is passed in (we dare not try to
    3581                 :            :  * compute it while holding exclusive buffer lock...)  NOTE that hot_attrs
    3582                 :            :  * is destructively modified!  That is OK since this is invoked at most once
    3583                 :            :  * by heap_update().
    3584                 :            :  *
    3585                 :            :  * Returns true if safe to do HOT update.
    3586                 :            :  */
    3587                 :            : static bool
    3588                 :            : HeapSatisfiesHOTUpdate(Relation relation, Bitmapset *hot_attrs,
    3589                 :            :                                            HeapTuple oldtup, HeapTuple newtup)
    3590                 :            : {
    3591                 :            :         int                     attrnum;
    3592                 :            : 
    3593         [ +  + ]:    1133198 :         while ((attrnum = bms_first_member(hot_attrs)) >= 0)
    3594                 :            :         {
    3595                 :            :                 /* Adjust for system attributes */
    3596                 :     815301 :                 attrnum += FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;
    3597                 :            : 
    3598                 :            :                 /* If the attribute value has changed, we can't do HOT update */
    3599 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     815301 :                 if (!heap_tuple_attr_equals(RelationGetDescr(relation), attrnum,
    3600                 :     815301 :                                                                         oldtup, newtup))
    3601                 :      10034 :                         return false;
    3602                 :            :         }
    3603                 :            : 
    3604                 :     317897 :         return true;
    3605                 :     327931 : }
    3606                 :            : 
    3607                 :            : /*
    3608                 :            :  *      simple_heap_update - replace a tuple
    3609                 :            :  *
    3610                 :            :  * This routine may be used to update a tuple when concurrent updates of
    3611                 :            :  * the target tuple are not expected (for example, because we have a lock
    3612                 :            :  * on the relation associated with the tuple).  Any failure is reported
    3613                 :            :  * via ereport().
    3614                 :            :  */
    3615                 :            : void
    3616                 :            : simple_heap_update(Relation relation, ItemPointer otid, HeapTuple tup)
    3617                 :            : {
    3618                 :     378476 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_DECLARE;
    3619                 :            : 
    3620                 :            :         HTSU_Result result;
    3621                 :            :         ItemPointerData update_ctid;
    3622                 :            :         TransactionId update_xmax;
    3623                 :            : 
    3624                 :     378476 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_ENTER;
    3625                 :            : 
    3626                 :     378476 :         result = heap_update_internal(
    3627                 :     378476 :                                                  relation, otid, tup,
    3628                 :            :                                                  &update_ctid, &update_xmax,
    3629                 :     378476 :                                                  GetCurrentCommandId(true), InvalidSnapshot,
    3630                 :            :                                                  true /* wait for commit */,
    3631                 :            :                                                  /* simple */ true);
    3632   [ -  +  -  - ]:     378476 :         switch (result)
    3633                 :            :         {
    3634                 :            :                 case HeapTupleSelfUpdated:
    3635                 :            :                         /* Tuple was already updated in current command? */
    3636                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "tuple already updated by self");
    3637                 :          0 :                         break;
    3638                 :            : 
    3639                 :            :                 case HeapTupleMayBeUpdated:
    3640                 :            :                         /* done successfully */
    3641                 :     378476 :                         break;
    3642                 :            : 
    3643                 :            :                 case HeapTupleUpdated:
    3644                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "tuple concurrently updated");
    3645                 :          0 :                         break;
    3646                 :            : 
    3647                 :            :                 default:
    3648                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "unrecognized heap_update status: %u", result);
    3649                 :          0 :                         break;
    3650                 :            :         }
    3651                 :            : 
    3652 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     378476 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_EXIT;
                 [ -  + ]
    3653                 :            : 
    3654                 :     378476 : }
    3655                 :            : 
    3656                 :            : /*
    3657                 :            :  *      heap_lock_tuple - lock a tuple in shared or exclusive mode
    3658                 :            :  *
    3659                 :            :  * Note that this acquires a buffer pin, which the caller must release.
    3660                 :            :  *
    3661                 :            :  * Input parameters:
    3662                 :            :  *      relation: relation containing tuple (caller must hold suitable lock)
    3663                 :            :  *      tuple->t_self: TID of tuple to lock (rest of struct need not be valid)
    3664                 :            :  *      cid: current command ID (used for visibility test, and stored into
    3665                 :            :  *              tuple's cmax if lock is successful)
    3666                 :            :  *      mode: indicates if shared or exclusive tuple lock is desired
    3667                 :            :  *      waittype:
    3668                 :            :  *              if LockTupleWait, wait for lock until it's acquired (normal behavior)
    3669                 :            :  *              if LockTupleNoWait,     if can't get lock right away, report error.
    3670                 :            :  *              if LockTupleIfNotLocked, if can't get lock right away, give up. no error
    3671                 :            :  *
    3672                 :            :  * Output parameters:
    3673                 :            :  *      *tuple: all fields filled in
    3674                 :            :  *      *buffer: set to buffer holding tuple (pinned but not locked at exit)
    3675                 :            :  *      *ctid: set to tuple's t_ctid, but only in failure cases
    3676                 :            :  *      *update_xmax: set to tuple's xmax, but only in failure cases
    3677                 :            :  *
    3678                 :            :  * Function result may be:
    3679                 :            :  *      HeapTupleMayBeUpdated: lock was successfully acquired
    3680                 :            :  *      HeapTupleSelfUpdated: lock failed because tuple updated by self
    3681                 :            :  *      HeapTupleUpdated: lock failed because tuple updated by other xact
    3682                 :            :  *
    3683                 :            :  * In the failure cases, the routine returns the tuple's t_ctid and t_xmax.
    3684                 :            :  * If t_ctid is the same as t_self, the tuple was deleted; if different, the
    3685                 :            :  * tuple was updated, and t_ctid is the location of the replacement tuple.
    3686                 :            :  * (t_xmax is needed to verify that the replacement tuple matches.)
    3687                 :            :  *
    3688                 :            :  *
    3689                 :            :  * NOTES: because the shared-memory lock table is of finite size, but users
    3690                 :            :  * could reasonably want to lock large numbers of tuples, we do not rely on
    3691                 :            :  * the standard lock manager to store tuple-level locks over the long term.
    3692                 :            :  * Instead, a tuple is marked as locked by setting the current transaction's
    3693                 :            :  * XID as its XMAX, and setting additional infomask bits to distinguish this
    3694                 :            :  * usage from the more normal case of having deleted the tuple.  When
    3695                 :            :  * multiple transactions concurrently share-lock a tuple, the first locker's
    3696                 :            :  * XID is replaced in XMAX with a MultiTransactionId representing the set of
    3697                 :            :  * XIDs currently holding share-locks.
    3698                 :            :  *
    3699                 :            :  * When it is necessary to wait for a tuple-level lock to be released, the
    3700                 :            :  * basic delay is provided by XactLockTableWait or MultiXactIdWait on the
    3701                 :            :  * contents of the tuple's XMAX.  However, that mechanism will release all
    3702                 :            :  * waiters concurrently, so there would be a race condition as to which
    3703                 :            :  * waiter gets the tuple, potentially leading to indefinite starvation of
    3704                 :            :  * some waiters.  The possibility of share-locking makes the problem much
    3705                 :            :  * worse --- a steady stream of share-lockers can easily block an exclusive
    3706                 :            :  * locker forever.      To provide more reliable semantics about who gets a
    3707                 :            :  * tuple-level lock first, we use the standard lock manager.  The protocol
    3708                 :            :  * for waiting for a tuple-level lock is really
    3709                 :            :  *              LockTuple()
    3710                 :            :  *              XactLockTableWait()
    3711                 :            :  *              mark tuple as locked by me
    3712                 :            :  *              UnlockTuple()
    3713                 :            :  * When there are multiple waiters, arbitration of who is to get the lock next
    3714                 :            :  * is provided by LockTuple().  However, at most one tuple-level lock will
    3715                 :            :  * be held or awaited per backend at any time, so we don't risk overflow
    3716                 :            :  * of the lock table.  Note that incoming share-lockers are required to
    3717                 :            :  * do LockTuple as well, if there is any conflict, to ensure that they don't
    3718                 :            :  * starve out waiting exclusive-lockers.  However, if there is not any active
    3719                 :            :  * conflict for a tuple, we don't incur any extra overhead.
    3720                 :            :  */
    3721                 :            : HTSU_Result
    3722                 :            : heap_lock_tuple(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple, Buffer *buffer,
    3723                 :            :                                 ItemPointer ctid, TransactionId *update_xmax,
    3724                 :            :                                 CommandId cid, LockTupleMode mode, LockTupleWaitType waittype)
    3725                 :            : {
    3726                 :      11088 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
    3727                 :            : 
    3728                 :            :         HTSU_Result result;
    3729                 :      11088 :         ItemPointer tid = &(tuple->t_self);
    3730                 :            :         ItemId          lp;
    3731                 :            :         PageHeader      dp;
    3732                 :            :         TransactionId xid;
    3733                 :            :         TransactionId xmax;
    3734                 :            :         uint16          old_infomask;
    3735                 :            :         uint16          new_infomask;
    3736                 :            :         LOCKMODE        tuple_lock_type;
    3737                 :      11088 :         bool            have_tuple_lock = false;
    3738                 :            : 
    3739                 :            :         // Fetch gp_persistent_relation_node information that will be added to XLOG record.
    3740                 :      11088 :         RelationFetchGpRelationNodeForXLog(relation);

    4025                 :            :                  * the xmax was a MultiXactId but it was not running anymore. There is
    4026                 :            :                  * a race condition, which is that the MultiXactId may have finished
    4027                 :            :                  * since then, but that uncommon case is handled within
    4028                 :            :                  * MultiXactIdExpand.

    4031                 :            :                  * regular TransactionId.  We test TransactionIdIsInProgress again

    4096                 :            :         /*
    4097                 :            :          * XLOG stuff.  You might think that we don't need an XLOG record because
    4098                 :            :          * there is no state change worth restoring after a crash.      You would be
    4099                 :            :          * wrong however: we have just written either a TransactionId or a
    4100                 :            :          * MultiXactId that may never have been seen on disk before, and we need
    4101                 :            :          * to make sure that there are XLOG entries covering those ID numbers.
    4102                 :            :          * Else the same IDs might be re-used after a crash, which would be
    4103                 :            :          * disastrous if this page made it to disk before the crash.  Essentially
    4104                 :            :          * we have to enforce the WAL log-before-data rule even in this case.
    4105                 :            :          * (Also, in a PITR log-shipping or 2PC environment, we have to have XLOG
    4106                 :            :          * entries for everything anyway.)
    4107                 :            :          */

    4151                 :            : 
    4152                 :            : 
    4153                 :            : /*
    4154                 :            :  * heap_inplace_update - update a tuple "in place" (ie, overwrite it)
    4155                 :            :  *
    4156                 :            :  * Overwriting violates both MVCC and transactional safety, so the uses
    4157                 :            :  * of this function in Postgres are extremely limited.  Nonetheless we
    4158                 :            :  * find some places to use it.
    4159                 :            :  *
    4160                 :            :  * The tuple cannot change size, and therefore it's reasonable to assume
    4161                 :            :  * that its null bitmap (if any) doesn't change either.  So we just
    4162                 :            :  * overwrite the data portion of the tuple without touching the null
    4163                 :            :  * bitmap or any of the header fields.
    4164                 :            :  *
    4165                 :            :  * tuple is an in-memory tuple structure containing the data to be written
    4166                 :            :  * over the target tuple.  Also, tuple->t_self identifies the target tuple.
    4167                 :            :  */
    4168                 :            : static void
    4169                 :            : heap_inplace_update_internal(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple, bool freeze)
    4170                 :            : {
    4171                 :     948694 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
    4172                 :            : 
    4173                 :            :         Buffer          buffer;
    4174                 :            :         Page            page;
    4175                 :            :         OffsetNumber offnum;
    4176                 :     948694 :         ItemId          lp = NULL;
    4177                 :            :         HeapTupleHeader htup;
    4178                 :            :         uint32          oldlen;
    4179                 :            :         uint32          newlen;
    4180                 :            : 
    4181                 :            :         /* 
    4182                 :            :          * Fetch gp_persistent_relation_node information that will be added
    4183                 :            :          * to XLOG record.
    4184                 :            :          */
    4185                 :     948694 :         RelationFetchGpRelationNodeForXLog(relation);
    4186                 :            :         
    4187                 :            :         // -------- MirroredLock ----------
    4188 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:     948694 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_LOCK;
         [ #  # ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    4189                 :            :         
    4190 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     948694 :         buffer = ReadBuffer(relation, ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&(tuple->t_self)));
         [ -  + ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    4191                 :     948694 :         LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
    4192 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     948694 :         page = (Page) BufferGetPage(buffer);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    4193                 :            : 
    4194 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     948694 :         offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(tuple->t_self));
                 [ -  + ]
    4195 [ -  + ][ +  - ]:     948694 :         if (PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) >= offnum)
    4196                 :     948694 :                 lp = PageGetItemId(page, offnum);
    4197                 :            : 
    4198 [ -  + ][ +  - ]:     948694 :         if (PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) < offnum || !ItemIdIsNormal(lp))
                 [ -  + ]
    4199                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "heap_inplace_update: invalid lp");
    4200                 :            : 
    4201 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     948694 :         htup = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lp);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
    4202                 :            : 
    4203                 :     948694 :         oldlen = ItemIdGetLength(lp) - htup->t_hoff;
    4204                 :     948694 :         newlen = tuple->t_len - tuple->t_data->t_hoff;
    4205 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     948694 :         if (oldlen != newlen || htup->t_hoff != tuple->t_data->t_hoff)
    4206                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "heap_inplace_update: wrong tuple length");
    4207                 :            : 
    4208                 :            :         /* NO EREPORT(ERROR) from here till changes are logged */
    4209         [ -  + ]:     948694 :         START_CRIT_SECTION();
    4210                 :            : 
    4211                 :     948694 :         memcpy((char *) htup + htup->t_hoff,
    4212                 :            :                    (char *) tuple->t_data + tuple->t_data->t_hoff,
    4213                 :            :                    newlen);
    4214                 :            : 
    4215                 :     948694 :         MarkBufferDirty(buffer);
    4216                 :            : 
    4217                 :            :         /* XLOG stuff */
    4218         [ +  - ]:     948694 :         if (!relation->rd_istemp)
    4219                 :            :         {
    4220                 :            :                 xl_heap_inplace xlrec;
    4221                 :            :                 XLogRecPtr      recptr;
    4222                 :            :                 XLogRecData rdata[2];
    4223                 :            :

    4225                 :            :

    4230                 :            :

    4236                 :            :

         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
         [ +  + ][ +  - ]
                 [ #  # ]

    4252                 :            :

    4255                 :     948694 :                 CacheInvalidateHeapTuple(relation, tuple);
    4256                 :     948694 : }
    4257                 :            : 
    4258                 :            : void
    4259                 :            : frozen_heap_inplace_update(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple)
    4260                 :            : {
    4261                 :     564127 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_DECLARE;
    4262                 :            :

    4266                 :            : 
    4267 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     564127 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_EXIT;
                 [ -  + ]
    4268                 :     564127 : }
    4269                 :            : 
    4270                 :            : void
    4271                 :            : heap_inplace_update(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple)
    4272                 :            : {
    4273                 :     384567 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_VERIFY_NO_LOCK_LEAK_DECLARE;

    4281                 :            : 
    4282                 :            : /*
    4283                 :            :  * heap_freeze_tuple
    4284                 :            :  *
    4285                 :            :  * Check to see whether any of the XID fields of a tuple (xmin, xmax, xvac)
    4286                 :            :  * are older than the specified cutoff XID.  If so, replace them with
    4287                 :            :  * FrozenTransactionId or InvalidTransactionId as appropriate, and return
    4288                 :            :  * TRUE.  Return FALSE if nothing was changed.
    4289                 :            :  *
    4290                 :            :  * It is assumed that the caller has checked the tuple with
    4291                 :            :  * HeapTupleSatisfiesVacuum() and determined that it is not HEAPTUPLE_DEAD
    4292                 :            :  * (else we should be removing the tuple, not freezing it).
    4293                 :            :  *
    4294                 :            :  * NB: cutoff_xid *must* be <= the current global xmin, to ensure that any
    4295                 :            :  * XID older than it could neither be running nor seen as running by any
    4296                 :            :  * open transaction.  This ensures that the replacement will not change
    4297                 :            :  * anyone's idea of the tuple state.  Also, since we assume the tuple is
    4298                 :            :  * not HEAPTUPLE_DEAD, the fact that an XID is not still running allows us
    4299                 :            :  * to assume that it is either committed good or aborted, as appropriate;
    4300                 :            :  * so we need no external state checks to decide what to do.  (This is good
    4301                 :            :  * because this function is applied during WAL recovery, when we don't have
    4302                 :            :  * access to any such state, and can't depend on the hint bits to be set.)
    4303                 :            :  *
    4304                 :            :  * In lazy VACUUM, we call this while initially holding only a shared lock
    4305                 :            :  * on the tuple's buffer.  If any change is needed, we trade that in for an
    4306                 :            :  * exclusive lock before making the change.  Caller should pass the buffer ID
    4307                 :            :  * if shared lock is held, InvalidBuffer if exclusive lock is already held.
    4308                 :            :  *
    4309                 :            :  * Note: it might seem we could make the changes without exclusive lock, since
    4310                 :            :  * TransactionId read/write is assumed atomic anyway.  However there is a race
    4311                 :            :  * condition: someone who just fetched an old XID that we overwrite here could
    4312                 :            :  * conceivably not finish checking the XID against pg_clog before we finish
    4313                 :            :  * the VACUUM and perhaps truncate off the part of pg_clog he needs.  Getting
    4314                 :            :  * exclusive lock ensures no other backend is in process of checking the
    4315                 :            :  * tuple status.  Also, getting exclusive lock makes it safe to adjust the
    4316                 :            :  * infomask bits.
    4317                 :            :  *
    4318                 :            :  * In GPDB, cutoff_xid is passed as pointer, as this function may change its
    4319                 :            :  * value. cutoff_xid passed in is just based on local visibility and hence may
    4320                 :            :  * not be met always. Function checks localxid from distributed aspect and may
    4321                 :            :  * not be able to freeze (remove) all xids lower than cutoff_xid if still
    4322                 :            :  * needed from distributed visibility perspective. So, for such cases the
    4323                 :            :  * cutoff_xid (freeze limit) is lowered and updated new lower value reflected
    4324                 :            :  * back to caller. Using the same caller can then correctly set *relfrozenxid*
    4325                 :            :  * of the relation. If this is not done, relation may have xids lower than
    4326                 :            :  * relfrozenxid which is wrong. As based on relfrozenxid, datfrozenxid gets
    4327                 :            :  * set, which finally controls cut-off point for clog or distributed commit
    4328                 :            :  * log. Not correctly reflecting relfrozenxid may end-up trying to read
    4329                 :            :  * non-existent clog files.
    4330                 :            :  */
    4331                 :            : bool
    4332                 :            : heap_freeze_tuple(HeapTupleHeader tuple, TransactionId *cutoff_xid,
    4333                 :            :                                   Buffer buf, bool xlog_replay)
    4334                 :            : {
    4335                 :     674342 :         bool            changed = false;
    4336                 :            :         TransactionId xid;
    4337                 :            : 
    4338         [ -  + ]:     674342 :         if (buf != InvalidBuffer)
    4339 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_MUST_ALREADY_BE_HELD;
    4340                 :            : 
    4341                 :     674342 :         xid = HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tuple);
    4342 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     674342 :         if (TransactionIdIsNormal(xid) &&
    4343                 :     674342 :                 TransactionIdPrecedes(xid, *cutoff_xid))
    4344                 :            :         {
    4345                 :            :                 /*
    4346                 :            :                  * Will consult distributed snapshot and freeze xmin only if globally
    4347                 :            :                  * can be seen by *all* the transactions. Hence, protects against case
    4348                 :            :                  * where xmin of tuple is lower than cutoff_xid but tuple may not be
    4349                 :            :                  * seen via some distributed snapshot. Missing to check the
    4350                 :            :                  * distributed perspective will go ahead to freeze the xmin, making
    4351                 :            :                  * the tuple visible to everyone yielding wrong result means breakage
    4352                 :            :                  * of snapshot isolation.
    4353                 :            :                  */
    4354 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:        149 :                 if (xlog_replay ||
    4355         [ +  + ]:        149 :                         (tuple->t_infomask2 & HEAP_XMIN_DISTRIBUTED_SNAPSHOT_IGNORE) ||
    4356                 :        102 :                         localXidSatisfiesAnyDistributedSnapshot(xid) == false)
    4357                 :            :                 {
    4358         [ -  + ]:        149 :                         if (buf != InvalidBuffer)
    4359                 :            :                         {
    4360                 :            :                                 /* trade in share lock for exclusive lock */
    4361                 :          0 :                                 LockBuffer(buf, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
    4362                 :          0 :                                 LockBuffer(buf, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
    4363                 :          0 :                                 buf = InvalidBuffer;
    4364                 :          0 :                         }
    4365                 :        149 :                         HeapTupleHeaderSetXmin(tuple, FrozenTransactionId);
    4366                 :            : 
    4367                 :            :                         /*
    4368                 :            :                          * Might as well fix the hint bits too; usually XMIN_COMMITTED will
    4369                 :            :                          * already be set here, but there's a small chance not.
    4370                 :            :                          */
    4371 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        149 :                         Assert(!(tuple->t_infomask & HEAP_XMIN_INVALID));
    4372                 :        149 :                         tuple->t_infomask |= HEAP_XMIN_COMMITTED;
    4373                 :        149 :                         changed = true;
    4374                 :        149 :                 }
    4375                 :            :                 else
    4376                 :            :                 {
    4377                 :          0 :                         *cutoff_xid = xid;
    4378                 :            :                 }
    4379                 :        149 :         }
    4380                 :            : 
    4381                 :            :         /*
    4382                 :            :          * When we release shared lock, it's possible for someone else to change
    4383                 :            :          * xmax before we get the lock back, so repeat the check after acquiring
    4384                 :            :          * exclusive lock.      (We don't need this pushup for xmin, because only
    4385                 :            :          * VACUUM could be interested in changing an existing tuple's xmin, and
    4386                 :            :          * there's only one VACUUM allowed on a table at a time.)
    4387                 :            :          */
    4388                 :            : recheck_xmax:
    4389         [ +  - ]:     674342 :         if (!(tuple->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI))
    4390                 :            :         {
    4391                 :     674342 :                 xid = HeapTupleHeaderGetXmax(tuple);
    4392 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:     674342 :                 if (TransactionIdIsNormal(xid) &&
    4393                 :      49810 :                         TransactionIdPrecedes(xid, *cutoff_xid))
    4394                 :            :                 {
    4395                 :            :                         /*
    4396                 :            :                          * Need to prevent case where due to distributed visibility the
    4397                 :            :                          * tuple is visible, but it may still have xmax lower than local
    4398                 :            :                          * cutoff_xid, in that case xmax shouldn't get treated as aborted
    4399                 :            :                          * transaction and marked as InvalidTransactionId. Hence, first
    4400                 :            :                          * check if xmax is committed or not. Only if committed and
    4401                 :            :                          * hintbit for tuple says distributed snapshot can be ignored
    4402                 :            :                          * which means globally there exists no snapshot with distributed
    4403                 :            :                          * xid lower than this xmax, this tuple can be freezed. This
    4404                 :            :                          * allows freezing of xmax for aborted delete scenarios in which
    4405                 :            :                          * case distributedness doesn't matter. Visibility checks or
    4406                 :            :                          * HeapTupleSatisfiesVacuum() called before reaching here will set
    4407                 :            :                          * this hintbit, allowing us to avoid consulting the distributed
    4408                 :            :                          * log again. Refer for further details
    4409                 :            :                          * DistributedSnapshotWithLocalMapping_CommittedTest. For xlog
    4410                 :            :                          * replay no need to check the hint-bits, just let the replay
    4411                 :            :                          * perform its duties.
    4412                 :            :                          */
    4413 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (xlog_replay ||
    4414         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 !(tuple->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_COMMITTED) ||
    4415                 :          0 :                                 (tuple->t_infomask2 & HEAP_XMAX_DISTRIBUTED_SNAPSHOT_IGNORE))
    4416                 :            :                         {
    4417         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (buf != InvalidBuffer)
    4418                 :            :                                 {
    4419                 :            :                                         /* trade in share lock for exclusive lock */
    4420                 :          0 :                                         LockBuffer(buf, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
    4421                 :          0 :                                         LockBuffer(buf, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
    4422                 :          0 :                                         buf = InvalidBuffer;
    4423                 :          0 :                                         goto recheck_xmax;              /* see comment above */
    4424                 :            :                                 }
    4425                 :          0 :                                 HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(tuple, InvalidTransactionId);
    4426                 :            : 
    4427                 :            :                                 /*
    4428                 :            :                                  * The tuple might be marked either XMAX_INVALID or XMAX_COMMITTED
    4429                 :            :                                  * + LOCKED.  Normalize to INVALID just to be sure no one gets
    4430                 :            :                                  * confused.
    4431                 :            :                                  */
    4432                 :          0 :                                 tuple->t_infomask &= ~HEAP_XMAX_COMMITTED;
    4433                 :          0 :                                 tuple->t_infomask |= HEAP_XMAX_INVALID;
    4434                 :          0 :                                 HeapTupleHeaderClearHotUpdated(tuple);
    4435                 :          0 :                                 changed = true;
    4436                 :          0 :                         }
    4437                 :            :                         else
    4438                 :            :                         {
    4439                 :          0 :                                 *cutoff_xid = xid;
    4440                 :            :                         }
    4441                 :          0 :                 }
    4442                 :     674342 :         }
    4443                 :            :         else
    4444                 :            :         {
    4445                 :            :                 /*----------
    4446                 :            :                  * XXX perhaps someday we should zero out very old MultiXactIds here?
    4447                 :            :                  *
    4448                 :            :                  * The only way a stale MultiXactId could pose a problem is if a
    4449                 :            :                  * tuple, having once been multiply-share-locked, is not touched by
    4450                 :            :                  * any vacuum or attempted lock or deletion for just over 4G MultiXact
    4451                 :            :                  * creations, and then in the probably-narrow window where its xmax
    4452                 :            :                  * is again a live MultiXactId, someone tries to lock or delete it.
    4453                 :            :                  * Even then, another share-lock attempt would work fine.  An
    4454                 :            :                  * exclusive-lock or delete attempt would face unexpected delay, or
    4455                 :            :                  * in the very worst case get a deadlock error.  This seems an
    4456                 :            :                  * extremely low-probability scenario with minimal downside even if
    4457                 :            :                  * it does happen, so for now we don't do the extra bookkeeping that
    4458                 :            :                  * would be needed to clean out MultiXactIds.
    4459                 :            :                  *----------
    4460                 :            :                  */
    4461                 :            :         }
    4462                 :            : 
    4463                 :            :         /*
    4464                 :            :          * Although xvac per se could only be set by VACUUM, it shares physical
    4465                 :            :          * storage space with cmax, and so could be wiped out by someone setting
    4466                 :            :          * xmax.  Hence recheck after changing lock, same as for xmax itself.
    4467                 :            :          */
    4468                 :            : recheck_xvac:
    4469         [ +  + ]:     674342 :         if (tuple->t_infomask & HEAP_MOVED)
    4470                 :            :         {
    4471         [ +  - ]:        634 :                 xid = HeapTupleHeaderGetXvac(tuple);
    4472 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:        634 :                 if (TransactionIdIsNormal(xid) &&
    4473                 :         38 :                         TransactionIdPrecedes(xid, *cutoff_xid))
    4474                 :            :                 {
    4475         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (buf != InvalidBuffer)
    4476                 :            :                         {
    4477                 :            :                                 /* trade in share lock for exclusive lock */
    4478                 :          0 :                                 LockBuffer(buf, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
    4479                 :          0 :                                 LockBuffer(buf, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
    4480                 :          0 :                                 buf = InvalidBuffer;
    4481                 :          0 :                                 goto recheck_xvac;              /* see comment above */
    4482                 :            :                         }
    4483                 :            : 
    4484                 :            :                         /*
    4485                 :            :                          * If a MOVED_OFF tuple is not dead, the xvac transaction must
    4486                 :            :                          * have failed; whereas a non-dead MOVED_IN tuple must mean the
    4487                 :            :                          * xvac transaction succeeded.
    4488                 :            :                          */
    4489         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (tuple->t_infomask & HEAP_MOVED_OFF)
    4490 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 HeapTupleHeaderSetXvac(tuple, InvalidTransactionId);
    4491                 :            :                         else
    4492 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 HeapTupleHeaderSetXvac(tuple, FrozenTransactionId);
    4493                 :            : 
    4494                 :            :                         /*
    4495                 :            :                          * Might as well fix the hint bits too; usually XMIN_COMMITTED
    4496                 :            :                          * will already be set here, but there's a small chance not.
    4497                 :            :                          */
    4498 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(!(tuple->t_infomask & HEAP_XMIN_INVALID));
    4499                 :          0 :                         tuple->t_infomask |= HEAP_XMIN_COMMITTED;
    4500                 :          0 :                         changed = true;
    4501                 :          0 :                 }
    4502                 :        634 :         }
    4503                 :            : 
    4504                 :     674342 :         return changed;
    4505                 :            : }
    4506                 :            : 
    4507                 :            : 
    4508                 :            : /* ----------------
    4509                 :            :  *              heap_markpos    - mark scan position
    4510                 :            :  * ----------------
    4511                 :            :  */
    4512                 :            : void
    4513                 :            : heap_markpos(HeapScanDesc scan)
    4514                 :            : {
    4515                 :            :         /* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
    4516                 :            : 
    4517         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (scan->rs_ctup.t_data != NULL)
    4518                 :            :         {
    4519                 :          0 :                 scan->rs_mctid = scan->rs_ctup.t_self;
    4520         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (scan->rs_pageatatime)
    4521                 :          0 :                         scan->rs_mindex = scan->rs_cindex;
    4522                 :          0 :         }
    4523                 :            :         else
    4524 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 ItemPointerSetInvalid(&scan->rs_mctid);
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
    4525                 :          0 : }

    4527                 :            : 
    4528                 :            : void
    4529                 :            : heap_markposx(HeapScanDesc scan, HeapTuple tuple)

                 [ #  # ]
    4534                 :          0 :                 scan->rs_mctid = tuple->t_self;
    4535         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (scan->rs_pageatatime)
    4536 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         scan->rs_mindex = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&tuple->t_self);
                 [ #  # ]
    4537                 :          0 :         }
    4538                 :            :         else
    4539                 :            :         {
    4540 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 ItemPointerSetInvalid(&scan->rs_mctid);
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
    4541                 :            :         }
    4542                 :          0 : }

    4545                 :            : /* ----------------

                 [ #  # ]

    4564                 :          0 :                 scan->rs_cblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
    4565                 :          0 :                 scan->rs_inited = false;

    4581                 :            :                                                                 NULL);
    4582                 :          0 :                 }
    4583                 :            :                 else
    4584                 :          0 :                         heapgettup(scan,
    4585                 :            :                                            NoMovementScanDirection,
    4586                 :            :                                            0,           /* needn't recheck scan keys */
    4587                 :            :                                            NULL);
    4588                 :            :         }
    4589                 :          0 : }

    4591                 :            : /*
