    4625                 :            : /*
    4626                 :            :  * Perform XLogInsert for a heap-clean operation.  Caller must already
    4627                 :            :  * have modified the buffer and marked it dirty.
    4628                 :            :  *
    4629                 :            :  * Note: prior to Postgres 8.3, the entries in the nowunused[] array were
    4630                 :            :  * zero-based tuple indexes.  Now they are one-based like other uses
    4631                 :            :  * of OffsetNumber.
    4632                 :            :  */
    4633                 :            : XLogRecPtr
    4634                 :            : log_heap_clean(Relation reln, Buffer buffer,
    4635                 :            :                            OffsetNumber *redirected, int nredirected,
    4636                 :            :                            OffsetNumber *nowdead, int ndead,
    4637                 :            :                            OffsetNumber *nowunused, int nunused,
    4638                 :            :                            bool redirect_move)
    4639                 :            : {
    4640                 :            :         xl_heap_clean xlrec;
    4641                 :            :         uint8           info;
    4642                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      recptr;
    4643                 :            :         XLogRecData rdata[4];
    4644                 :            : 
    4645                 :            :         /* Caller should not call me on a temp relation */
    4646 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      15309 :         Assert(!reln->rd_istemp);
    4647                 :            : 
    4648                 :      15309 :         xl_heapnode_set(&xlrec.heapnode, reln);
    4649                 :      15309 :         xlrec.block = BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer);
    4650                 :      15309 :         xlrec.nredirected = nredirected;
    4651                 :      15309 :         xlrec.ndead = ndead;
    4652                 :            : 
    4653                 :      15309 :         rdata[0].data = (char *) &xlrec;
    4654                 :      15309 :         rdata[0].len = SizeOfHeapClean;
    4655                 :      15309 :         rdata[0].buffer = InvalidBuffer;
    4656                 :      15309 :         rdata[0].next = &(rdata[1]);
    4657                 :            : 
    4658                 :            :         /*
    4659                 :            :          * The OffsetNumber arrays are not actually in the buffer, but we pretend
    4660                 :            :          * that they are.  When XLogInsert stores the whole buffer, the offset
    4661                 :            :          * arrays need not be stored too.  Note that even if all three arrays are
    4662                 :            :          * empty, we want to expose the buffer as a candidate for whole-page
    4663                 :            :          * storage, since this record type implies a defragmentation operation
    4664                 :            :          * even if no item pointers changed state.
    4665                 :            :          */
    4666         [ +  + ]:      15309 :         if (nredirected > 0)
    4667                 :            :         {
    4668                 :       5736 :                 rdata[1].data = (char *) redirected;
    4669                 :       5736 :                 rdata[1].len = nredirected * sizeof(OffsetNumber) * 2;
    4670                 :       5736 :         }
    4671                 :            :         else
    4672                 :            :         {
    4673                 :       9573 :                 rdata[1].data = NULL;
    4674                 :       9573 :                 rdata[1].len = 0;
    4675                 :            :         }
    4676                 :      15309 :         rdata[1].buffer = buffer;
    4677                 :      15309 :         rdata[1].buffer_std = true;
    4678                 :      15309 :         rdata[1].next = &(rdata[2]);
    4679                 :            : 
    4680         [ +  + ]:      15309 :         if (ndead > 0)
    4681                 :            :         {
    4682                 :      13122 :                 rdata[2].data = (char *) nowdead;
    4683                 :      13122 :                 rdata[2].len = ndead * sizeof(OffsetNumber);
    4684                 :      13122 :         }
    4685                 :            :         else
    4686                 :            :         {
    4687                 :       2187 :                 rdata[2].data = NULL;
    4688                 :       2187 :                 rdata[2].len = 0;
    4689                 :            :         }
    4690                 :      15309 :         rdata[2].buffer = buffer;
    4691                 :      15309 :         rdata[2].buffer_std = true;
    4692                 :      15309 :         rdata[2].next = &(rdata[3]);
    4693                 :            : 
    4694         [ +  + ]:      15309 :         if (nunused > 0)
    4695                 :            :         {
    4696                 :       5614 :                 rdata[3].data = (char *) nowunused;
    4697                 :       5614 :                 rdata[3].len = nunused * sizeof(OffsetNumber);
    4698                 :       5614 :         }

    4702                 :       9695 :                 rdata[3].len = 0;

    4705                 :      15309 :         rdata[3].buffer_std = true;
    4706                 :      15309 :         rdata[3].next = NULL;

    4710                 :            :

    4714                 :            : /*

    4719                 :            : log_heap_freeze(Relation reln, Buffer buffer,

    4728 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         40 :         Assert(!reln->rd_istemp);

    4763                 :            : /*
    4764                 :            :  * Perform XLogInsert for a heap-update operation.      Caller must already
    4765                 :            :  * have modified the buffer(s) and marked them dirty.
    4766                 :            :  */
    4767                 :            : static XLogRecPtr
    4768                 :            : log_heap_update(Relation reln, Buffer oldbuf, ItemPointerData from,
    4769                 :            :                                 Buffer newbuf, HeapTuple newtup, bool move)
    4770                 :            : {
    4771                 :            :         /*
    4772                 :            :          * Note: xlhdr is declared to have adequate size and correct alignment for
    4773                 :            :          * an xl_heap_header.  However the two tids, if present at all, will be
    4774                 :            :          * packed in with no wasted space after the xl_heap_header; they aren't

    4778                 :            :         {

    4787                 :            :         XLogRecData rdata[4];
    4788 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     404971 :         Page            page = BufferGetPage(newbuf);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    4789                 :            : 
    4790                 :            :         /* Caller should not call me on a temp relation */
    4791 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     404971 :         Assert(!reln->rd_istemp);
    4792                 :            : 
    4793                 :     404971 :         xl_heaptid_set(&xlrec.target, reln, &from);
    4794                 :            : 
    4795         [ +  + ]:     404971 :         if (move)
    4796                 :            :         {
    4797 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       2190 :                 Assert(!HeapTupleIsHeapOnly(newtup));
    4798                 :       2190 :                 info = XLOG_HEAP_MOVE;
    4799                 :       2190 :         }
    4800         [ +  + ]:     402781 :         else if (HeapTupleIsHeapOnly(newtup))
    4801                 :     317876 :                 info = XLOG_HEAP_HOT_UPDATE;

    4804                 :            : 
    4805                 :     404971 :         xlrec.target.node = reln->rd_node;

    4807                 :     404971 :         xlrec.newtid = newtup->t_self;
    4808                 :            : 
    4809                 :     404971 :         rdata[0].data = (char *) &xlrec;

    4811                 :     404971 :         rdata[0].buffer = InvalidBuffer;
    4812                 :     404971 :         rdata[0].next = &(rdata[1]);
    4813                 :            : 
    4814                 :     404971 :         rdata[1].data = NULL;
    4815                 :     404971 :         rdata[1].len = 0;
    4816                 :     404971 :         rdata[1].buffer = oldbuf;
    4817                 :     404971 :         rdata[1].buffer_std = true;

    4819                 :            :

    4822                 :     404971 :         xlhdr.hdr.t_hoff = newtup->t_data->t_hoff;

         [ -  + ][ +  + ]

    4868                 :            : /*
    4869                 :            :  * Perform XLogInsert for a heap-move operation.  Caller must already

    4872                 :            : XLogRecPtr

    4879                 :            : 
    4880                 :            : /*
    4881                 :            :  * Insert HEAP_NEWPAGE record into XLOG.  Caller is responsible for providing a
    4882                 :            :  * xl_heap_newpage record with valid blkno, persistent TID, persistent serial
    4883                 :            :  * number, and relfilenode set.
    4884                 :            :  *
    4885                 :            :  * Note: all current callers build pages in private memory and write them
    4886                 :            :  * directly to smgr, rather than using bufmgr.  Therefore there is no need
    4887                 :            :  * to pass a buffer ID to XLogInsert, nor to perform MarkBufferDirty within
    4888                 :            :  * the critical section.
    4889                 :            :  *
    4890                 :            :  * Note: the NEWPAGE log record is used for both heaps and indexes, so do
    4891                 :            :  * not do anything that assumes we are touching a heap.
    4892                 :            :  */
    4893                 :            : static XLogRecPtr
    4894                 :            : log_newpage_internal(xl_heap_newpage *xlrec, Page page)
    4895                 :            : 
    4896                 :            : {
    4897                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      recptr;
    4898                 :            :         XLogRecData rdata[2];
    4899                 :            : 
    4900 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      55104 :         Assert(!RelFileNode_IsEmpty(&xlrec->heapnode.node));
    4901 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      55104 :         Assert(BlockNumberIsValid(xlrec->blkno));
    4902 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:      55104 :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode() && !gp_before_persistence_work)
    4903                 :            :         {
    4904 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:      55104 :                 Assert(ItemPointerIsValid(&xlrec->heapnode.persistentTid));
                 [ -  + ]
    4905 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      55104 :                 Assert(xlrec->heapnode.persistentSerialNum);
    4906                 :      55104 :         }
    4907                 :            : 
    4908                 :            :         /* NO ELOG(ERROR) from here till newpage op is logged */
    4909         [ -  + ]:      55104 :         START_CRIT_SECTION();
    4910                 :            : 
    4911                 :      55104 :         rdata[0].data = (char *) xlrec;
    4912                 :      55104 :         rdata[0].len = SizeOfHeapNewpage;
    4913                 :      55104 :         rdata[0].buffer = InvalidBuffer;
    4914                 :      55104 :         rdata[0].next = &(rdata[1]);
    4915                 :            : 
    4916                 :      55104 :         rdata[1].data = (char *) page;
    4917                 :      55104 :         rdata[1].len = BLCKSZ;
    4918                 :      55104 :         rdata[1].buffer = InvalidBuffer;
    4919                 :      55104 :         rdata[1].next = NULL;
    4920                 :            : 
    4921                 :      55104 :         recptr = XLogInsert(RM_HEAP_ID, XLOG_HEAP_NEWPAGE, rdata);
    4922                 :            : 
    4923                 :            :         /*
    4924                 :            :          * The page may be uninitialized. If so, we can't set the LSN
    4925                 :            :          * and TLI because that would corrupt the page.
    4926                 :            :          */
    4927         [ +  - ]:      55104 :         if (!PageIsNew(page))
    4928                 :            :         {
    4929                 :      55104 :                 PageSetLSN(page, recptr);

    6011                 :            : 
    6012                 :            : /*
    6013                 :            :  *      heap_sync               - sync a heap, for use when no WAL has been written
    6014                 :            :  *
    6015                 :            :  * This forces the heap contents (including TOAST heap if any) down to disk.
    6016                 :            :  * If we skipped using WAL, and it's not a temp relation, we must force the
    6017                 :            :  * relation down to disk before it's safe to commit the transaction.  This
    6018                 :            :  * requires writing out any dirty buffers and then doing a forced fsync.
    6019                 :            :  *
    6020                 :            :  * Indexes are not touched.  (Currently, index operations associated with
    6021                 :            :  * the commands that use this are WAL-logged and so do not need fsync.
    6022                 :            :  * That behavior might change someday, but in any case it's likely that
    6023                 :            :  * any fsync decisions required would be per-index and hence not appropriate
    6024                 :            :  * to be done here.)
    6025                 :            :  */
    6026                 :            : void
    6027                 :            : heap_sync(Relation rel)
    6028                 :            : {
    6029                 :            :         /* temp tables never need fsync */
    6030         [ -  + ]:        222 :         if (rel->rd_istemp)
    6031                 :          0 :                 return;
    6032                 :            : 
    6033                 :            :         /* main heap */
    6034                 :        222 :         FlushRelationBuffers(rel);
    6035                 :            :         /* FlushRelationBuffers will have opened rd_smgr */
    6036                 :        222 :         smgrimmedsync(rel->rd_smgr);
    6037                 :            : 
    6038                 :            :         /* toast heap, if any */
    6039         [ +  + ]:        222 :         if (OidIsValid(rel->rd_rel->reltoastrelid))
    6040                 :            :         {
    6041                 :            :                 Relation        toastrel;
    6042                 :            : 
    6043                 :        202 :                 toastrel = heap_open(rel->rd_rel->reltoastrelid, AccessShareLock);
    6044                 :        202 :                 FlushRelationBuffers(toastrel);
    6045                 :        202 :                 smgrimmedsync(toastrel->rd_smgr);
    6046                 :        202 :                 heap_close(toastrel, AccessShareLock);
    6047                 :        202 :         }
    6048                 :        222 : }
    6049                 :            : 
    6050                 :            : /*
    6051                 :            :  * Function checks if its okay to generate xlog record for the relation in
    6052                 :            :  * question. It does same from perspective in GPDB need to have persistent
    6053                 :            :  * table information to be added to xlog records. So, for tables where PT info
    6054                 :            :  * is not needed can proceed, but for relations where PT info is must for xlog
    6055                 :            :  * record, if it can be fetched then fetches and signal go ahead else just
    6056                 :            :  * returns xlog should not be generated.
    6057                 :            :  */
    6058                 :            : bool
    6059                 :            : RelationAllowedToGenerateXLogRecord(Relation relation)
    6060                 :            : {
    6061         [ -  + ]:     293032 :         if (InRecovery)
    6062                 :          0 :                 return false;
    6063                 :            : 
    6064         [ +  + ]:     293032 :         if (GpPersistent_SkipXLogInfo(relation->rd_id))
    6065                 :        327 :                 return true;
    6066                 :            : 
    6067                 :            :         /*
    6068                 :            :          * Only for non-system table fetch the info if not present. Incase of
    6069                 :            :          * system table like pg_class if trying to fetch the informatiom here it
    6070                 :            :          * may lead to infinite recursion as for reading pg_class tuple will have
    6071                 :            :          * to read gp_relation_node tuple to get PT info for which need to read
    6072                 :            :          * back pg_class tuple, hence avoid the same.
    6073                 :            :          */
    6074         [ +  + ]:     292705 :         if (relation->rd_id >= FirstNormalObjectId)
    6075                 :       1826 :                 RelationFetchGpRelationNodeForXLog(relation);
    6076                 :            : 
    6077 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:     292705 :         if (relation->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.isPresent &&
    6078                 :     252988 :                 !relation->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.tidAllowedToBeZero)
    6079                 :     252988 :                 return true;
    6080                 :            : 
    6081                 :      39717 :         return false;
    6082                 :     293032 : }
